bro dive deep in the internet what metrics is possible to get via GH api... i bet it is possible to get waaaaaaaaaay much more than you mentioned.. and we wil need to create Complex Metrics when we get all   
  metrics from JIRA and GH                                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  or you can try to make real requst ot the GH and JIRA to check what is possible

input.description: Deep research: all available GitHub API metrics
input.prompt:
Research exhaustively what data/metrics are available from the GitHub REST API and GraphQL API that would be useful for a developer performance tracking platform. 

Go through the official GitHub API docs at https://docs.github.com/en/rest and https://docs.github.com/en/graphql

I need a comprehensive, categorized list of every data point available. Think about:

1. Commits — what fields, what aggregate endpoints exist
2. Pull Requests — every field available (size, reviews, comments, requested reviewers, labels, draft status, merge method, linked issues, review threads, etc.)
3. Code Reviews — review state, review comments, reaction counts, time to review
4. Issues — all fields, relationships to PRs
5. Repositories — stats endpoints (traffic, clones, views, contributors, punch card, commit activity, code frequency, participation)
6. Users/Contributors — profile data, contribution graphs, follower counts, repository stats
7. Actions/CI — workflow runs, job durations, failure rates
8. Releases — frequency, assets
9. Projects/Milestones
10. Discussions
11. GraphQL-only data not available in REST
12. Any rate limiting considerations

Focus specifically on what's useful for MEASURING developer performance, collaboration quality, code quality signals, and productivity patterns. Be thorough — the user specifically said "waaaay more than you mentioned."

Return a structured comprehensive list grouped by category with notes on what's REST vs GraphQL only.

input.description: Deep research: all available Jira API metrics
input.prompt:
Research exhaustively what data/metrics are available from the Jira REST API (both Jira Cloud and Jira Server/Data Center) that would be useful for a developer performance tracking platform.

Check the official Jira REST API docs at https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/ and related Atlassian developer docs.

I need a comprehensive categorized list of every data point available. Think about:

1. Issues — every field (status, priority, type, reporter, assignee, components, labels, story points, time tracking, sprint, etc.)
2. Issue transitions/history — full changelog, status transition times, who changed what
3. Worklogs — time logged per user per issue
4. Sprints — sprint data, velocity, completion rates, scope changes mid-sprint
5. Boards — board configuration, columns, swimlanes
6. Versions/Releases — release dates, issues included
7. Components — ownership, issue counts
8. Users — account info, group membership
9. Comments — count, authors, timing
10. Issue links — dependencies, blocks, relates-to
11. Epics — epic-level aggregation
12. Custom fields — common ones (story points, acceptance criteria, etc.)
13. Agile metrics — velocity, burndown data
14. Time in status — how long issues sat in each column
15. Cycle time, lead time — what raw data enables these calculations

Focus on what's useful for measuring: developer productivity, estimation accuracy, sprint health, ticket quality, bottlenecks, cycle time, and individual contribution to delivery.

Be thorough — return a structured comprehensive list grouped by category with API endpoint references where relevant.

content.text:
Now I have enough data from across all the major API surfaces. Let me compile the comprehensive reference.

---

# GitHub API Data Points for Developer Performance Tracking: Comprehensive Reference

---

## 1. COMMITS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits` — List commits (filterable by `sha`, `path`, `author`, `committer`, `since`, `until`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}` — Get single commit (full detail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{commit_sha}/pulls` — PRs associated with a commit
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{commit_sha}/branches-where-head` — Branches where this commit is HEAD
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/compare/{basehead}` — Compare two refs (`ahead_by`, `behind_by`, `total_commits`, `status`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/statuses` — CI statuses on a commit
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/status` — Combined status (aggregate pass/fail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/commits/{commit_sha}` — Low-level git commit object

### Commit Object Fields
- `sha`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `commit.message` — full message text (subject + body)
- `commit.author.name`, `.email`, `.date` — **authored timestamp** (different from committed timestamp)
- `commit.committer.name`, `.email`, `.date` — **committed timestamp**
- `commit.comment_count` — number of comments on this commit
- `commit.tree.sha` — tree SHA
- `commit.verification.verified` (boolean), `.reason`, `.signature`, `.payload`, `.verified_at` — GPG/SSH signature verification
- `author` (Simple User or null) — GitHub account if matched by email
- `committer` (Simple User or null)
- `parents` array — SHAs of parent commits (multiple = merge commit)
- **`stats.additions`**, **`stats.deletions`**, **`stats.total`** — ONLY on single-commit endpoint, NOT on list
- `files[]` — array of Diff Entry objects per file:
  - `filename`, `previous_filename` (on rename)
  - `status`: `added`, `removed`, `modified`, `renamed`, `copied`, `changed`, `unchanged`
  - `additions`, `deletions`, `changes`
  - `patch` — unified diff text
  - `blob_url`, `raw_url`, `contents_url`

### Compare Endpoint Fields
- `status`: `diverged`, `ahead`, `behind`, `identical`
- `ahead_by`, `behind_by`, `total_commits`
- `base_commit`, `merge_base_commit`
- `commits[]`, `files[]`

### Important Notes
- Stats (additions/deletions) require N+1 calls — one per commit — when processing a list. Budget accordingly.
- Merge commits have 2+ parents; you can detect merges by `parents.length > 1`.
- `author.date` vs `committer.date` differ when commits are rebased or cherry-picked.
- List endpoint max: 100 per page; no server-side aggregation.

---

## 2. PULL REQUESTS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` — List PRs (`state`, `head`, `base`, `sort`, `direction`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}` — Get single PR (full detail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/commits` — Commits in the PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/files` — Files changed (Diff Entry objects)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/merge` — Check if merged (204/404)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews` — All reviews
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/requested_reviewers` — Requested reviewers + teams
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}/pulls` — PRs containing a commit

### PR Object Fields (REST)
**Identity & State:**
- `number`, `id`, `node_id`, `state` (`open`, `closed`), `locked`
- `title`, `body`
- `draft` — boolean, PR is a draft
- `merged` — boolean
- `author_association` — `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, `COLLABORATOR`, `CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIMER`, `NONE`

**Timestamps (critical for time-to-merge, cycle time calculations):**
- `created_at` — PR opened
- `updated_at` — last activity
- `closed_at` — closed (merged or abandoned)
- `merged_at` — when merged

**Size Metrics:**
- `additions`, `deletions`, `changed_files`, `commits` — counts

**Review & Comment Counts:**
- `review_comments` — count of inline review comments
- `comments` — count of issue-style (general) comments

**Merge Details:**
- `mergeable` — boolean or null (GitHub is computing)
- `rebaseable` — boolean or null
- `mergeable_state` — `clean`, `blocked`, `behind`, `unstable`, `has_hooks`, `dirty`, `unknown`
- `merge_commit_sha`
- `merged_by` — Simple User
- `auto_merge` — object with `enabled_by`, `merge_method` (`merge`, `squash`, `rebase`), `commit_title`, `commit_message`
- `maintainer_can_modify`

**Branch Context:**
- `head.ref`, `head.sha`, `head.repo`, `head.label`
- `base.ref`, `base.sha`, `base.repo`, `base.label`

**Participants:**
- `user` — PR author (Simple User)
- `assignees[]` — assigned users
- `requested_reviewers[]` — user review requests
- `requested_teams[]` — team review requests
- `labels[]` — label objects (name, color, description)
- `milestone` — Milestone object

**Links:**
- `commits_url`, `review_comments_url`, `comments_url`, `statuses_url`, `issue_url`
- `active_lock_reason`

### GraphQL-Only PR Fields
The GraphQL `PullRequest` type exposes fields not in REST:
- `reviewDecision` — enum: `APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, `REVIEW_REQUIRED`
- `mergeStateStatus` — `CLEAN`, `BLOCKED`, `DIRTY`, `DRAFT`, `HAS_HOOKS`, `UNKNOWN`, `UNSTABLE`
- `timelineItems` — full ordered event stream (reviews, comments, commits, labels, assignments, status changes, etc.)
- `reviewThreads` — connection to all review threads (each thread: `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, `isCollapsed`, `resolvedBy`, `path`, `line`, `originalLine`, `diffSide`, `startLine`, `startDiffSide`)
- `closingIssuesReferences` — issues that this PR will close on merge
- `totalCommentsCount` — combined review + issue comments count
- `viewerCanMergeAsAdmin` — can the viewer bypass branch protections
- `potentialMergeCommit` — what the merge commit would be
- `baseRefOid`, `headRefOid` — full commit SHAs
- `headRef`, `baseRef` — full branch ref objects
- `changedFiles`, `additions`, `deletions` — also available in GraphQL
- `isDraft` — same as REST `draft` but explicit
- `isReadByViewer`
- `commits` connection — paginated with `CommitEdge` including `headCommit.status` (check runs, statuses)
- `files` connection — changed files with pagination
- `participants` — all users who participated (not just assignees)
- `suggestedReviewers` — GitHub-suggested reviewers based on history
- `autoMergeRequest` — full auto-merge configuration object
- `checksResourcePath` / `checksUrl` — link to checks page
- `isCrossRepository` — fork PR detection
- `canBeRebased` — rebase eligibility
- `resourcePath`, `url`

---

## 3. CODE REVIEWS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews` — All reviews on a PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews/{review_id}` — Single review
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews/{review_id}/comments` — Comments from a specific review
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments` — List review comments across all PRs in repo
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/comments` — Review comments on a PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments/{comment_id}` — Single review comment

### Review Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `user` — reviewer (Simple User)
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `state` — `APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, `COMMENTED`, `PENDING`, `DISMISSED`
- `submitted_at` — **critical for time-to-review calculation**
- `commit_id` — SHA of commit being reviewed
- `author_association`
- `pull_request_url`

### Review Comment (Inline) Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `diff_hunk` — surrounding code context from the diff
- `path` — file path
- `position` — position in the diff (deprecated)
- `original_position` — original position
- `line`, `original_line` — actual line numbers
- `start_line`, `original_start_line` — for multi-line comments
- `side` — `LEFT` (old version) or `RIGHT` (new version)
- `start_side`
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `in_reply_to_id` — thread reply structure
- `pull_request_review_id` — which review this belongs to
- `user`, `author_association`
- `reactions` — Reaction Rollup: `+1`, `-1`, `laugh`, `confused`, `heart`, `hooray`, `eyes`, `rocket`
- `commit_id`, `original_commit_id`
- `resolved` — boolean (REST)
- `subject_type` — `line` or `file`

### GraphQL Review Thread Fields
Via `pullRequest.reviewThreads`:
- `isResolved`, `resolvedBy` (User)
- `isOutdated` — comment is on stale code
- `isCollapsed`
- `path`, `line`, `originalLine`
- `diffSide`, `startLine`, `startDiffSide`
- `viewerCanResolve`, `viewerCanUnresolve`
- `comments` — paginated comment connection

### Performance-Relevant Derived Metrics
- **Time to first review** = `review.submitted_at` − `pr.created_at`
- **Review turnaround** per reviewer from `review.submitted_at`
- **Review cycles** = count of CHANGES_REQUESTED → push → re-review sequences
- **Thread resolution rate** = resolved threads / total threads
- **Comment-to-approval ratio** = `review_comments` / number of APPROVED reviews
- **Reviewer responsiveness** = time between `review_requested_at` (from timeline) and `review.submitted_at`

---

## 4. ISSUES

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` — List issues (filter: `state`, `labels`, `assignee`, `milestone`, `since`, `sort`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}` — Get single issue
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/comments` — Issue comments
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/events` — Issue events (labeled, assigned, milestoned, etc.)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/timeline` — Full timeline events
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments` — All issue comments in repo
- `GET /orgs/{org}/issues` — Issues assigned to authenticated user across org

### Issue Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `number`, `html_url`
- `title`, `body`, `body_text`, `body_html`
- `state` — `open`, `closed`
- `state_reason` — `completed`, `reopened`, `not_planned`, `duplicate`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`
- `user` — issue author
- `closed_by` — who closed it
- `assignees[]`
- `labels[]`
- `milestone`
- `locked`, `active_lock_reason`
- `comments` — count
- `reactions` — `+1`, `-1`, `laugh`, `confused`, `heart`, `hooray`, `eyes`, `rocket`
- `author_association`
- `pull_request` — present if issue is linked to a PR (has `merged_at`)
- `timeline_url`
- `performed_via_github_app`
- `draft` (boolean)
- `type` — Issue Type object
- `parent_issue_url`, `sub_issues_summary` (`total`, `completed`, `percent_completed`)
- `issue_dependencies_summary` (`blocked_by`, `blocking` counts)
- `issue_field_values` — custom field values

### Issue Timeline Event Types
- `labeled` / `unlabeled` — label changes
- `assigned` / `unassigned` — assignee changes
- `milestoned` / `demilestoned`
- `renamed` — title change (from/to)
- `closed` / `reopened`
- `locked` / `unlocked`
- `referenced` — referenced from a commit
- `cross-referenced` — referenced from another issue/PR
- `review_requested` / `review_request_removed`
- `review_dismissed`
- `committed` — commit added to PR
- `reviewed` — review submitted
- `line-commented`, `commit-commented`
- `added_to_project` / `moved_column_in_project` / `removed_from_project`
- `converted_note_to_issue`
- `state_change` (open/close)

### Issue Comment Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `user`, `author_association`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `reactions` (full Reaction Rollup)
- `performed_via_github_app`

---

## 5. REPOSITORY STATISTICS & TRAFFIC

### Statistics Endpoints (cached, async — may return 202 while computing)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/contributors`
  - Per contributor: `author` (Simple User), `total` (all-time commits), `weeks[]` with `w` (Unix timestamp), `a` (additions), `d` (deletions), `c` (commits)
  - **Note**: excludes merge commits

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/commit_activity`
  - Last 52 weeks: `week` (Unix timestamp), `total`, `days[]` (Sun–Sat array of commit counts)

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/code_frequency`
  - Weekly additions/deletions since repo creation: `[week_unix_timestamp, additions, deletions]`
  - **Note**: limited to repos with <10,000 commits

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/participation`
  - Last 52 weeks: `all[]` (total commits), `owner[]` (commits by repo owner)
  - Useful for owner vs. community contribution split

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/punch_card`
  - Hourly distribution: `[day_of_week (0=Sun), hour (0–23), commit_count]`
  - Great for visualizing work patterns / time zone analysis

### Traffic Endpoints (require push access, 14-day window only)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/views` — `count` (total), `uniques`, `views[]` with `timestamp`, `count`, `uniques`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/clones` — same structure as views
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/popular/referrers` — top 10: `referrer`, `count`, `uniques`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/popular/paths` — top 10: `path`, `title`, `count`, `uniques`

### Languages
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages` — object: `{ "TypeScript": 432891, "Python": 12345 }` (bytes per language)

### Repository Object (key fields for performance platform)
- `size` — repo size in KB
- `stargazers_count`, `forks_count`, `watchers_count`, `open_issues_count`
- `default_branch`
- `language` — primary language
- `topics[]`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `pushed_at`
- `visibility` — `public`, `private`, `internal`
- `archived`, `disabled`
- Merge strategy flags: `allow_merge_commit`, `allow_squash_merge`, `allow_rebase_merge`, `allow_auto_merge`, `delete_branch_on_merge`
- `merge_commit_title`, `merge_commit_message`, `squash_merge_commit_title`, `squash_merge_commit_message`
- `security_and_analysis` — advanced security, secret scanning, dependency review status
- `has_issues`, `has_projects`, `has_wiki`, `has_discussions`
- `web_commit_signoff_required`

---

## 6. USERS & CONTRIBUTORS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /users/{username}` — Public user profile
- `GET /user` — Authenticated user (more fields)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contributors` — List contributors with `contributions` count
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators` — With permission levels
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/{username}/permission` — Exact permission + role
- `GET /orgs/{org}/members` — Org members (filter by `role: admin|member`)
- `GET /orgs/{org}/teams/{team_slug}/members` — Team members

### User Profile Fields
- `login`, `id`, `node_id`, `type`, `site_admin`
- `name`, `company`, `blog`, `location`, `email`, `hireable`, `bio`, `twitter_username`
- `public_repos`, `public_gists`
- `followers`, `following`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `avatar_url`, `html_url`
- `plan.name`, `plan.space`, `plan.private_repos`, `plan.collaborators` (authenticated only)
- `total_private_repos`, `owned_private_repos`, `private_gists`, `disk_usage`, `collaborators` (authenticated only)
- `two_factor_authentication` (authenticated only)

### GraphQL User Fields (additional)
Via `query { user(login: "...") }`:
- `contributionsCollection(from: ..., to: ...)` — time-bounded contribution data:
  - `totalCommitContributions`
  - `totalPullRequestContributions`
  - `totalIssueContributions`
  - `totalPullRequestReviewContributions`
  - `totalRepositoriesWithContributedCommits`
  - `totalRepositoriesWithContributedIssues`
  - `totalRepositoriesWithContributedPullRequests`
  - `totalRepositoriesWithContributedPullRequestReviews`
  - `restrictedContributionsCount` — contributions to private repos viewer cannot access
  - `commitContributionsByRepository[]` — per-repo breakdown
  - `pullRequestContributionsByRepository[]`
  - `issueContributionsByRepository[]`
  - `pullRequestReviewContributionsByRepository[]`
  - `contributionCalendar` — full heatmap grid
  - `hasAnyContributions`, `hasAnyRestrictedContributions`
- `pullRequests(states: [...])` — paginated PR connection
- `issues(states: [...])` — paginated issue connection
- `repositories(ownerAffiliations: [...])` — repos with affiliation type
- `repositoriesContributedTo` — repos contributed to (not owned)
- `status` — user's current status message
- `projectsV2` — Projects v2 membership
- `socialAccounts` — linked social accounts
- `packages` — published packages

### `CreatedCommitContribution` fields (per day per repo, GraphQL)
- `commitCount` — commits on that day to that repo
- `occurredAt` — date
- `repository` — repository object
- `isRestricted` — private repo viewer can't access

---

## 7. GITHUB ACTIONS / CI

### Workflow Runs
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` — List runs (filter: `actor`, `branch`, `event`, `status`, `created`, `head_sha`, `exclude_pull_requests`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` — Single run
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/timing` — Billable usage

**Workflow Run Object Fields:**
- `id`, `node_id`, `name`, `run_number`, `run_attempt`
- `status`: `queued`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `waiting`, `requested`, `pending`
- `conclusion`: `success`, `failure`, `cancelled`, `neutral`, `skipped`, `timed_out`, `action_required`, `startup_failure`
- `event` — triggering event: `push`, `pull_request`, `schedule`, `workflow_dispatch`, `release`, etc.
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `run_started_at` — **run_started_at is the actual start, created_at is when queued**
- `head_sha`, `head_branch`
- `workflow_id`, `path` — workflow file path
- `actor`, `triggering_actor` — who triggered
- `check_suite_id`, `check_suite_node_id`
- `pull_requests[]` — associated PRs
- `repository`, `head_repository`
- `cancel_url`, `rerun_url`, `previous_attempt_url`, `jobs_url`, `logs_url`, `artifacts_url`

**Run Timing Fields** (`/timing` endpoint):
- `billable.UBUNTU.total_ms`, `.jobs`, `.job_runs[]` (job_id + duration_ms)
- `billable.MACOS.total_ms` (etc.)
- `billable.WINDOWS.total_ms` (etc.)
- `run_duration_ms` — total wall-clock time

### Workflow Jobs
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/jobs` — Jobs in a run
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}` — Single job

**Job Object Fields:**
- `id`, `run_id`, `node_id`, `name`
- `status`, `conclusion` (same enums as workflow run)
- `created_at`, `started_at`, `completed_at` — **derive duration = completed_at − started_at**
- `head_sha`, `head_branch`, `workflow_name`
- `runner_id`, `runner_name`, `runner_group_id`, `runner_group_name`
- `run_attempt`, `run_url`, `check_run_url`
- `labels[]` — runner labels
- `steps[]`:
  - `name`, `number`
  - `status`, `conclusion`
  - `started_at`, `completed_at` — **step-level timing**

### Workflows
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows` — List workflows
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/runs` — Runs for a specific workflow

### Actions Cache
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/caches` — Individual cache entries: `id`, `key`, `ref`, `version`, `created_at`, `last_accessed_at`, `size_in_bytes`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/cache/usage` — `active_caches_count`, `active_caches_size_in_bytes`
- `GET /orgs/{org}/actions/cache/usage-by-repository` — Per-repo cache usage

### Artifacts
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/artifacts` — All artifacts
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/artifacts` — Artifacts for a run
- Fields: `name`, `size_in_bytes`, `expired`, `created_at`, `expires_at`, `digest`, `workflow_run.head_sha`, `.head_branch`

### Check Runs (CI Integration Layer)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/check-runs` — All check runs for a commit
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/check-suites/{check_suite_id}/check-runs`

**Check Run Fields:**
- `id`, `name`, `head_sha`
- `status`: `queued`, `in_progress`, `completed`, `waiting`, `requested`, `pending`
- `conclusion`: `success`, `failure`, `neutral`, `cancelled`, `skipped`, `timed_out`, `action_required`
- `started_at`, `completed_at` — duration
- `output.title`, `.summary`, `.text`, `.annotations_count`, `.annotations_url`
- `check_suite.id`
- `app` — which app created the check (Codecov, SonarQube, etc.)
- `pull_requests[]` — associated PRs

### Commit Statuses (legacy CI)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/statuses`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/status` — Combined
- Fields: `state` (`error`, `failure`, `pending`, `success`), `description`, `context` (CI system name), `target_url`, `creator`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

---

## 8. RELEASES

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases` — List releases
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/{release_id}` — Single release
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest` — Latest published release
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/tags/{tag}` — By tag

### Release Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `tag_name`, `name`
- `target_commitish` — branch/commit the tag points to
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `draft`, `prerelease`
- `created_at`, `published_at`, `updated_at`
- `author` — Simple User
- `assets[]` — release assets: `name`, `size`, `download_count`, `content_type`, `state`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `uploader`
- `assets_url`, `tarball_url`, `zipball_url`, `upload_url`
- `reactions` — Reaction Rollup
- `mentions_count`
- `discussion_url` — linked discussion

### Performance-Relevant Derived Metrics
- Release frequency (cadence) = count of non-draft non-prerelease releases over time
- Release size = `assets[].size` sum
- Asset download counts = popularity signal

---

## 9. PROJECTS & MILESTONES

### Milestones (REST)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/milestones` — List milestones
- Fields: `number`, `title`, `description`, `state` (`open`/`closed`), `due_on`, `open_issues`, `closed_issues`, `creator`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`
- **Progress**: `closed_issues / (open_issues + closed_issues)`

### Projects v2 (REST — limited, most data is GraphQL-only)
- `GET /orgs/{org}/projectsV2` — List projects
- `GET /orgs/{org}/projectsV2/{project_number}` — Single project
- Fields: `title`, `description`, `short_description`, `public`, `state` (`open`/`closed`), `owner`, `creator`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `is_template`, `latest_status_update` (`INACTIVE`, `ON_TRACK`, `AT_RISK`, `OFF_TRACK`, `COMPLETE`)

### Projects v2 (GraphQL — full access)
Via `repository.projectsV2` or `organization.projectsV2`:
- `ProjectV2Item` — each card: `content` (Issue/PR/DraftIssue), `type`, `isArchived`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
- `fieldValues` — custom field values (text, number, date, single-select, iteration, assignees, labels, milestone, repository, reviewers)
- Custom field types: `ProjectV2Field`, `ProjectV2SingleSelectField`, `ProjectV2IterationField`
- `ProjectV2View` — view configurations
- `statusUpdates` — status history with `status`, `bodyText`, `createdAt`, `creator`

---

## 10. DISCUSSIONS (GraphQL Only — no REST API)

All discussion data is GraphQL-only:

```graphql
repository(owner: "...", name: "...") {
  discussions(first: 100) {
    nodes {
      id, number, title, body
      category { name, isAnswerable }
      author { login }
      createdAt, updatedAt, closedAt
      comments { totalCount }
      answerChosenAt, answerChosenBy { login }
      upvoteCount, locked, stateReason
    }
  }
}
```

**Key Discussion Fields:**
- `title`, `body`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `closedAt`
- `category.name`, `category.isAnswerable`
- `author`
- `comments.totalCount`
- `answerChosenAt`, `answerChosenBy` — for Q&A categories
- `upvoteCount`, `reactionGroups`
- `locked`, `stateReason` (`DUPLICATE`, `OUTDATED`, `RESOLVED`)
- `activeLockReason`

### Discussion Comments (GraphQL)
- `body`, `author`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
- `isAnswer` — whether this comment is the accepted answer
- `replyCount`, `reactions`

---

## 11. SECURITY & CODE QUALITY SIGNALS

### Code Scanning Alerts
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/analyses`
- Alert fields: `number`, `state` (`open`, `dismissed`, `fixed`), `severity` (`critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low`, `warning`, `note`, `error`), `rule.id`, `rule.name`, `rule.security_severity_level`, `rule.tags[]`, `tool.name`, `tool.version`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `fixed_at`, `dismissed_reason` (`false positive`, `won't fix`, `used in tests`), `dismissed_by`, `dismissed_at`, `dismissal_approved_by`, `dismissed_comment`, `most_recent_instance.location` (path, line/column), `most_recent_instance.classifications` (`source`, `generated`, `test`, `library`), `assignees[]`
- Analysis fields: `ref`, `commit_sha`, `tool.name`, `tool.version`, `results_count`, `rules_count`, `created_at`, `analysis_key`, `category`, `sarif_id`, `deletable`

### Secret Scanning Alerts
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/secret-scanning/alerts`
- Fields: `number`, `state` (`open`, `resolved`), `secret_type`, `secret_type_display_name`, `provider`, `validity` (`active`, `inactive`, `unknown`), `resolution` (`false_positive`, `wont_fix`, `revoked`, `used_in_tests`), `resolved_at`, `resolved_by`, `push_protection_bypassed` (boolean), `push_protection_bypassed_by`, `push_protection_bypassed_at`, `publicly_leaked`, `multi_repo`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

### Dependabot Alerts
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts`
- `GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/alerts`
- Fields: `number`, `state` (`auto_dismissed`, `dismissed`, `fixed`, `open`), `severity` (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`), `package.ecosystem`, `package.name`, `vulnerable_version_range`, `first_patched_version`, `dismissed_reason` (`fix_started`, `inaccurate`, `no_bandwidth`, `not_used`, `tolerable_risk`), `dismissed_by`, `dismissed_at`, `auto_dismissed_at`, `fixed_at`, `dependency.scope` (`development`/`runtime`), `security_advisory.ghsa_id`, `security_advisory.cve_id`, `security_advisory.severity`, `assignees[]`

### Dependency Review (per PR/comparison)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependency-graph/compare/{basehead}`
- Fields per dependency: `change_type` (`added`/`removed`), `manifest`, `ecosystem`, `name`, `version`, `scope`, `vulnerabilities[]` (each with `severity`, `advisory_ghsa_id`, `advisory_summary`, `advisory_url`)

---

## 12. DEPLOYMENTS & ENVIRONMENTS

### Deployments
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` — filter by `sha`, `ref`, `task`, `environment`
- Fields: `id`, `sha`, `ref`, `task` (e.g., `deploy`, `deploy:migrations`), `environment`, `original_environment`, `description`, `creator`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `transient_environment`, `production_environment`, `payload`

### Deployment Statuses
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{deployment_id}/statuses`
- Fields: `state` (`error`, `failure`, `pending`, `in_progress`, `queued`, `success`), `description`, `environment_url`, `log_url`, `creator`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

### Environments
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/environments` — Lists all environments
- Fields: `name`, `protection_rules[]` (required reviewers, wait timers, branch policies), `deployment_branch_policy`

---

## 13. EVENTS API (Activity Stream)

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/events` — Repo events (last 300, 30 days max)
- `GET /users/{username}/events` — User's public events
- `GET /orgs/{org}/events` — Org events

**Event Types relevant to performance tracking:**
- `PushEvent` — `payload.commits[]` with `sha`, `message`, `author`, `distinct`; `payload.size` (commit count)
- `PullRequestEvent` — action: `opened`, `closed`, `merged`, `edited`, `review_requested`, `ready_for_review`, `converted_to_draft`
- `PullRequestReviewEvent` — `payload.review.state`, `payload.review.submitted_at`
- `PullRequestReviewCommentEvent`
- `IssuesEvent` — action: `opened`, `closed`, `reopened`, `assigned`, `labeled`
- `IssueCommentEvent`
- `CreateEvent` — branch/tag creation: `payload.ref_type`, `payload.ref`
- `DeleteEvent` — branch/tag deletion
- `ReleaseEvent` — `payload.action: published`
- `CommitCommentEvent`
- `ForkEvent` — `payload.forkee`
- `WatchEvent` — star added
- `MemberEvent` — collaborator added
- `GollumEvent` — wiki changes
- `DiscussionEvent`

**Important limitation**: max 300 events, 30-day window. For historical analysis you must use the dedicated endpoints.

---

## 14. GRAPHQL-ONLY DATA (No REST Equivalent)

These are only accessible via the GraphQL API at `https://api.github.com/graphql`:

| Feature | GraphQL Query Path | Key Data |
|---|---|---|
| Contribution heatmap | `user.contributionsCollection` | Full 52-week grid, per-repo breakdown |
| PR review decision | `pullRequest.reviewDecision` | `APPROVED` / `CHANGES_REQUESTED` / `REVIEW_REQUIRED` |
| PR timeline items | `pullRequest.timelineItems` | Full ordered event stream |
| PR review threads | `pullRequest.reviewThreads` | isResolved, isOutdated, path, line |
| PR closing issues | `pullRequest.closingIssuesReferences` | Issues that will close on merge |
| PR suggested reviewers | `pullRequest.suggestedReviewers` | ML-recommended reviewers |
| Discussion data | `repository.discussions` | Entire discussions feature |
| Merge queue status | `pullRequest.isInMergeQueue` | Position in merge queue |
| Projects v2 items | `projectV2.items` | Field values, custom fields, iterations |
| Security advisory | `securityAdvisory(ghsaId: ...)` | Full GHSA advisory data |
| Sponsor data | `user.sponsorshipsAsMaintainer` | Sponsorship relationships |
| User status | `user.status` | Current availability status |
| Repo vulnerability alerts | `repository.vulnerabilityAlerts` | Dependabot alerts via GraphQL |
| Packages | `repository.packages` | Published packages (npm, Docker, etc.) |
| Deploy keys | `repository.deployKeys` | |
| Branch protection rules | `repository.branchProtectionRules` | Required reviews, status checks, restrictions |
| Ruleset configurations | `repository.rulesets` | Fine-grained branch/tag rules |

---

## 15. SEARCH API

### Endpoints
- `GET /search/commits` — Search commits (default branch only)
- `GET /search/issues` — Search issues AND pull requests
- `GET /search/code` — Search code/file contents
- `GET /search/repositories`
- `GET /search/users`
- `GET /search/labels`
- `GET /search/topics`

### Useful Qualifiers for Performance Tracking

**Commit search:**
- `author:username`, `committer:username`
- `author-date:>2024-01-01`
- `repo:owner/name`
- `merge:false` — exclude merge commits

**Issue/PR search:**
- `is:pr is:merged merged:2024-01-01..2024-12-31`
- `author:username`, `assignee:username`, `reviewed-by:username`
- `review:approved`, `review:changes_requested`, `review:none`
- `involves:username`
- `label:bug`, `milestone:v1.0`
- `no:assignee`, `no:milestone`
- `comments:>5`
- `reactions:>10`

**Code search:**
- `language:typescript`
- `repo:owner/name`
- `path:src/` — limit to directory
- `filename:.env` — detect risky files

**Rate limit**: 10 req/min (unauthenticated), 30 req/min (authenticated). Max 1,000 results per query.

---

## 16. RATE LIMITING

### REST API Limits
| User Type | Requests/Hour | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Authenticated user (PAT) | 5,000 | |
| GitHub App installation | 5,000 min, scales to 12,500 | +50/hr per repo >20 |
| Enterprise Cloud user | 15,000 | |
| GitHub App (Enterprise Cloud) | 15,000 | |
| Unauthenticated | 60 | By IP |

**Secondary rate limits (per-minute caps):**
- Max 100 concurrent requests
- Max 900 points/minute on REST
- Max 90s CPU per 60s real-time
- Max 80 content-generating requests/minute
- Max 2,000 OAuth token requests/hour

**Response headers:** `x-ratelimit-limit`, `x-ratelimit-remaining`, `x-ratelimit-used`, `x-ratelimit-reset`, `x-ratelimit-resource`

### GraphQL API Limits
| User Type | Points/Hour |
|---|---|
| Individual user | 5,000 |
| Enterprise Cloud user | 10,000 |
| GitHub App installation | 5,000 (scales with +50/hr per entity >20) |
| GitHub Actions (GITHUB_TOKEN) | 1,000/hr per repo |

**Secondary**: Max 2,000 points/minute. Mutations cost 5 points; queries cost 1 point.
**Node limit**: Max 500,000 nodes per request. `first`/`last` must be 1–100.

### Strategic Implications for a Performance Platform
- **Never** call single-commit detail endpoint for every commit in a list — you'll burn rate limit instantly. Use stats endpoints instead.
- Use **GraphQL batching**: fetch PR + reviews + commits + check runs in a single query
- Use **`since`/`until`** filters on commit list to narrow payloads
- **Webhooks** are your best friend for real-time ingestion — no polling cost
- Cache stats endpoints aggressively — they're computed async and cached by GitHub
- For org-wide data: prefer org-level endpoints over looping through repos
- Use **conditional requests** (`If-None-Match` / `If-Modified-Since`) — 304 responses don't count against rate limit

---

## 17. WEBHOOKS (Event-Driven Alternative to Polling)

Rather than polling, subscribe to webhooks for real-time ingestion:

**Most relevant events for performance tracking:**
- `push` — commit data (includes `commits[]` with sha, message, author, added/removed/modified files)
- `pull_request` — all PR lifecycle events
- `pull_request_review` — review submitted
- `pull_request_review_comment` — inline comment
- `pull_request_review_thread` — thread resolved/unresolved
- `issues` — issue events
- `issue_comment`
- `workflow_run` — CI run completed
- `workflow_job` — job completed (includes `started_at`, `completed_at`, `conclusion`)
- `check_run`, `check_suite`
- `deployment`, `deployment_status`
- `release`
- `milestone`
- `project_v2_item` — project card moves

Webhook payloads mirror the REST response fields above.

---

## KEY FIELDS BY USE CASE (Quick Reference)

**Cycle time / Lead time:**
- PR `created_at` → `merged_at` (time in review)
- First commit date in branch → PR `merged_at` (full cycle)
- Issue `created_at` → closing commit date (issue resolution time)

**Code churn / PR size:**
- `pr.additions`, `pr.deletions`, `pr.changed_files`, `pr.commits`
- Per-file: diff entry `additions`, `deletions`, `status`

**Review quality:**
- `review.state` distribution per reviewer
- `review_comments` count vs. `reviews` count
- Review thread `isResolved` rate
- Time between `review_requested` (timeline event) and `review.submitted_at`

**CI health:**
- Workflow run `conclusion` rates (success/failure/cancelled)
- `run_duration_ms` trends
- Job `started_at` → `completed_at` per runner
- Step-level timing for bottleneck detection

**Security hygiene:**
- Code scanning `severity` distribution, `fixed_at` presence
- Secret scanning `push_protection_bypassed` counts
- Dependabot `severity`, time-to-fix (`fixed_at` − `created_at`)
- Dependency review `vulnerabilities[]` on each PR diff

**Collaboration patterns:**
- `requested_reviewers` response time (GraphQL timeline)
- `author_association` of commenters
- `PullRequestReviewEvent` frequency per contributor
- Punch card data for work-hour analysis

Sources:
- [Objects - GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/reference/objects)
- [GitHub GraphQL API documentation - GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/graphql)
- [REST API endpoints for review requests - GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/review-requests)

content.text:
Now I have enough data from across all the major API surfaces. Let me compile the comprehensive reference.

---

# GitHub API Data Points for Developer Performance Tracking: Comprehensive Reference

---

## 1. COMMITS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits` — List commits (filterable by `sha`, `path`, `author`, `committer`, `since`, `until`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}` — Get single commit (full detail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{commit_sha}/pulls` — PRs associated with a commit
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{commit_sha}/branches-where-head` — Branches where this commit is HEAD
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/compare/{basehead}` — Compare two refs (`ahead_by`, `behind_by`, `total_commits`, `status`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/statuses` — CI statuses on a commit
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref}/status` — Combined status (aggregate pass/fail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/commits/{commit_sha}` — Low-level git commit object

### Commit Object Fields
- `sha`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `commit.message` — full message text (subject + body)
- `commit.author.name`, `.email`, `.date` — **authored timestamp** (different from committed timestamp)
- `commit.committer.name`, `.email`, `.date` — **committed timestamp**
- `commit.comment_count` — number of comments on this commit
- `commit.tree.sha` — tree SHA
- `commit.verification.verified` (boolean), `.reason`, `.signature`, `.payload`, `.verified_at` — GPG/SSH signature verification
- `author` (Simple User or null) — GitHub account if matched by email
- `committer` (Simple User or null)
- `parents` array — SHAs of parent commits (multiple = merge commit)
- **`stats.additions`**, **`stats.deletions`**, **`stats.total`** — ONLY on single-commit endpoint, NOT on list
- `files[]` — array of Diff Entry objects per file:
  - `filename`, `previous_filename` (on rename)
  - `status`: `added`, `removed`, `modified`, `renamed`, `copied`, `changed`, `unchanged`
  - `additions`, `deletions`, `changes`
  - `patch` — unified diff text
  - `blob_url`, `raw_url`, `contents_url`

### Compare Endpoint Fields
- `status`: `diverged`, `ahead`, `behind`, `identical`
- `ahead_by`, `behind_by`, `total_commits`
- `base_commit`, `merge_base_commit`
- `commits[]`, `files[]`

### Important Notes
- Stats (additions/deletions) require N+1 calls — one per commit — when processing a list. Budget accordingly.
- Merge commits have 2+ parents; you can detect merges by `parents.length > 1`.
- `author.date` vs `committer.date` differ when commits are rebased or cherry-picked.
- List endpoint max: 100 per page; no server-side aggregation.

---

## 2. PULL REQUESTS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` — List PRs (`state`, `head`, `base`, `sort`, `direction`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}` — Get single PR (full detail)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/commits` — Commits in the PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/files` — Files changed (Diff Entry objects)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/merge` — Check if merged (204/404)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews` — All reviews
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/requested_reviewers` — Requested reviewers + teams
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}/pulls` — PRs containing a commit

### PR Object Fields (REST)
**Identity & State:**
- `number`, `id`, `node_id`, `state` (`open`, `closed`), `locked`
- `title`, `body`
- `draft` — boolean, PR is a draft
- `merged` — boolean
- `author_association` — `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, `COLLABORATOR`, `CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR`, `FIRST_TIMER`, `NONE`

**Timestamps (critical for time-to-merge, cycle time calculations):**
- `created_at` — PR opened
- `updated_at` — last activity
- `closed_at` — closed (merged or abandoned)
- `merged_at` — when merged

**Size Metrics:**
- `additions`, `deletions`, `changed_files`, `commits` — counts

**Review & Comment Counts:**
- `review_comments` — count of inline review comments
- `comments` — count of issue-style (general) comments

**Merge Details:**
- `mergeable` — boolean or null (GitHub is computing)
- `rebaseable` — boolean or null
- `mergeable_state` — `clean`, `blocked`, `behind`, `unstable`, `has_hooks`, `dirty`, `unknown`
- `merge_commit_sha`
- `merged_by` — Simple User
- `auto_merge` — object with `enabled_by`, `merge_method` (`merge`, `squash`, `rebase`), `commit_title`, `commit_message`
- `maintainer_can_modify`

**Branch Context:**
- `head.ref`, `head.sha`, `head.repo`, `head.label`
- `base.ref`, `base.sha`, `base.repo`, `base.label`

**Participants:**
- `user` — PR author (Simple User)
- `assignees[]` — assigned users
- `requested_reviewers[]` — user review requests
- `requested_teams[]` — team review requests
- `labels[]` — label objects (name, color, description)
- `milestone` — Milestone object

**Links:**
- `commits_url`, `review_comments_url`, `comments_url`, `statuses_url`, `issue_url`
- `active_lock_reason`

### GraphQL-Only PR Fields
The GraphQL `PullRequest` type exposes fields not in REST:
- `reviewDecision` — enum: `APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, `REVIEW_REQUIRED`
- `mergeStateStatus` — `CLEAN`, `BLOCKED`, `DIRTY`, `DRAFT`, `HAS_HOOKS`, `UNKNOWN`, `UNSTABLE`
- `timelineItems` — full ordered event stream (reviews, comments, commits, labels, assignments, status changes, etc.)
- `reviewThreads` — connection to all review threads (each thread: `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, `isCollapsed`, `resolvedBy`, `path`, `line`, `originalLine`, `diffSide`, `startLine`, `startDiffSide`)
- `closingIssuesReferences` — issues that this PR will close on merge
- `totalCommentsCount` — combined review + issue comments count
- `viewerCanMergeAsAdmin` — can the viewer bypass branch protections
- `potentialMergeCommit` — what the merge commit would be
- `baseRefOid`, `headRefOid` — full commit SHAs
- `headRef`, `baseRef` — full branch ref objects
- `changedFiles`, `additions`, `deletions` — also available in GraphQL
- `isDraft` — same as REST `draft` but explicit
- `isReadByViewer`
- `commits` connection — paginated with `CommitEdge` including `headCommit.status` (check runs, statuses)
- `files` connection — changed files with pagination
- `participants` — all users who participated (not just assignees)
- `suggestedReviewers` — GitHub-suggested reviewers based on history
- `autoMergeRequest` — full auto-merge configuration object
- `checksResourcePath` / `checksUrl` — link to checks page
- `isCrossRepository` — fork PR detection
- `canBeRebased` — rebase eligibility
- `resourcePath`, `url`

---

## 3. CODE REVIEWS

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews` — All reviews on a PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews/{review_id}` — Single review
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/reviews/{review_id}/comments` — Comments from a specific review
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments` — List review comments across all PRs in repo
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/comments` — Review comments on a PR
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments/{comment_id}` — Single review comment

### Review Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `user` — reviewer (Simple User)
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `state` — `APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, `COMMENTED`, `PENDING`, `DISMISSED`
- `submitted_at` — **critical for time-to-review calculation**
- `commit_id` — SHA of commit being reviewed
- `author_association`
- `pull_request_url`

### Review Comment (Inline) Object Fields
- `id`, `node_id`, `html_url`
- `diff_hunk` — surrounding code context from the diff
- `path` — file path
- `position` — position in the diff (deprecated)
- `original_position` — original position
- `line`, `original_line` — actual line numbers
- `start_line`, `original_start_line` — for multi-line comments
- `side` — `LEFT` (old version) or `RIGHT` (new version)
- `start_side`
- `body`, `body_html`, `body_text`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `in_reply_to_id` — thread reply structure
- `pull_request_review_id` — which review this belongs to
- `user`, `author_association`
- `reactions` — Reaction Rollup: `+1`, `-1`, `laugh`, `confused`, `heart`, `hooray`, `eyes`, `rocket`
- `commit_id`, `original_commit_id`
- `resolved` — boolean (REST)
- `subject_type` — `line` or `file`

### GraphQL Review Thread Fields
Via `pullRequest.reviewThreads`:
- `isResolved`, `resolvedBy` (User)
- `isOutdated` — comment is on stale code
- `isCollapsed`
- `path`, `line`, `originalLine`
- `diffSide`, `startLine`, `startDiffSide`
- `viewerCanResolve`, `viewerCanUnresolve`
- `comments` — paginated comment connection

### Performance-Relevant Derived Metrics
- **Time to first review** = `review.submitted_at` − `pr.created_at`
- **Review turnaround** per reviewer from `review.submitted_at`
- **Review cycles** = count of CHANGES_REQUESTED → push → re-review sequences
- **Thread resolution rate** = resolved threads / total threads
- **Comment-to-approval ratio** = `review_comments` / number of APPROVED reviews
- **Reviewer responsiveness** = time between `review_requested_at` (from timeline) and `review.submitted_at`

---

## 4. ISSUES

### REST Endpoints
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues` — List issues (filter: `state`, `labels`, `assignee`, `milestone`, `since`, `sort`)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}` — Get single issue
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/comments` — Issue comments
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issue_number}/events` — Issue events (labeled, assigned, milestoned, etc.)

## 5. REPOSITORY STATISTICS & TRAFFIC

### Statistics Endpoints (cached, async — may return 202 while computing)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/contributors`
  - Per contributor: `author` (Simple User), `total` (all-time commits), `weeks[]` with `w` (Unix timestamp), `a` (additions), `d` (deletions), `c` (commits)
  - **Note**: excludes merge commits

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/commit_activity`
  - Last 52 weeks: `week` (Unix timestamp), `total`, `days[]` (Sun–Sat array of commit counts)

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/code_frequency`
  - Weekly additions/deletions since repo creation: `[week_unix_timestamp, additions, deletions]`
  - **Note**: limited to repos with <10,000 commits

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/participation`
  - Last 52 weeks: `all[]` (total commits), `owner[]` (commits by repo owner)
  - Useful for owner vs. community contribution split

- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/stats/punch_card`
  - Hourly distribution: `[day_of_week (0=Sun), hour (0–23), commit_count]`
  - Great for visualizing work patterns / time zone analysis

### Traffic Endpoints (require push access, 14-day window only)
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/views` — `count` (total), `uniques`, `views[]` with `timestamp`, `count`, `uniques`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/clones` — same structure as views
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/popular/referrers` — top 10: `referrer`, `count`, `uniques`
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/traffic/popular/paths` — top 10: `path`, `title`, `count`, `uniques`

### Languages
- `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages` — object: `{ "TypeScript": 432891, "Python": 12345 }` (bytes per language)

### Repository Object (key fields for performance platform)
- `size` — repo size in KB
- `stargazers_count`, `forks_count`, `watchers_count`, `open_issues_count`
- `default_branch`
- `language` — primary language
- `topics[]`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `pushed_at`
- `visibility` — `public`, `private`, `internal`
- `archived`, `disabled`
- Merge strategy flags: `allow_merge_commit`, `allow_squash_merge`, `allow_rebase_merge`, `allow_auto_merge`, `delete_branch_on_merge`
- `merge_commit_title`, `merge_commit_message`, `squash_merge_commit_title`, `squash_merge_commit_message`
- `security_and_analysis` — advanced security, secret scanning, dependency review status
- `has_issues`, `has_projects`, `has_wiki`, `has_discussions`
- `web_commit_signoff_required`

## 15. SEARCH API

### Endpoints
- `GET /search/commits` — Search commits (default branch only)
- `GET /search/issues` — Search issues AND pull requests
- `GET /search/code` — Search code/file contents
- `GET /search/repositories`
- `GET /search/users`
- `GET /search/labels`
- `GET /search/topics`

### Useful Qualifiers for Performance Tracking

**Commit search:**
- `author:username`, `committer:username`
- `author-date:>2024-01-01`
- `repo:owner/name`
- `merge:false` — exclude merge commits

**Issue/PR search:**
- `is:pr is:merged merged:2024-01-01..2024-12-31`
- `author:username`, `assignee:username`, `reviewed-by:username`
- `review:approved`, `review:changes_requested`, `review:none`
- `involves:username`
- `label:bug`, `milestone:v1.0`
- `no:assignee`, `no:milestone`
- `comments:>5`
- `reactions:>10`

**Code search:**
- `language:typescript`
- `repo:owner/name`
- `path:src/` — limit to directory
- `filename:.env` — detect risky files

**Rate limit**: 10 req/min (unauthenticated), 30 req/min (authenticated). Max 1,000 results per query.

## 16. RATE LIMITING

### REST API Limits
| User Type | Requests/Hour | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Authenticated user (PAT) | 5,000 | |
| GitHub App installation | 5,000 min, scales to 12,500 | +50/hr per repo >20 |
| Enterprise Cloud user | 15,000 | |
| GitHub App (Enterprise Cloud) | 15,000 | |
| Unauthenticated | 60 | By IP |

**Secondary rate limits (per-minute caps):**
- Max 100 concurrent requests
- Max 900 points/minute on REST
- Max 90s CPU per 60s real-time
- Max 80 content-generating requests/minute
- Max 2,000 OAuth token requests/hour

**Response headers:** `x-ratelimit-limit`, `x-ratelimit-remaining`, `x-ratelimit-used`, `x-ratelimit-reset`, `x-ratelimit-resource`

### GraphQL API Limits
| User Type | Points/Hour |
|---|---|
| Individual user | 5,000 |
| Enterprise Cloud user | 10,000 |
| GitHub App installation | 5,000 (scales with +50/hr per entity >20) |
| GitHub Actions (GITHUB_TOKEN) | 1,000/hr per repo |

**Secondary**: Max 2,000 points/minute. Mutations cost 5 points; queries cost 1 point.
**Node limit**: Max 500,000 nodes per request. `first`/`last` must be 1–100.

### Strategic Implications for a Performance Platform
- **Never** call single-commit detail endpoint for every commit in a list — you'll burn rate limit instantly. Use stats endpoints instead.
- Use **GraphQL batching**: fetch PR + reviews + commits + check runs in a single query
- Use **`since`/`until`** filters on commit list to narrow payloads
- **Webhooks** are your best friend for real-time ingestion — no polling cost
- Cache stats endpoints aggressively — they're computed async and cached by GitHub
- For org-wide data: prefer org-level endpoints over looping through repos
- Use **conditional requests** (`If-None-Match` / `If-Modified-Since`) — 304 responses don't count against rate limit

---

## 17. WEBHOOKS (Event-Driven Alternative to Polling)

Rather than polling, subscribe to webhooks for real-time ingestion:

**Most relevant events for performance tracking:**
- `push` — commit data (includes `commits[]` with sha, message, author, added/removed/modified files)
- `pull_request` — all PR lifecycle events
- `pull_request_review` — review submitted
- `pull_request_review_comment` — inline comment
- `pull_request_review_thread` — thread resolved/unresolved
- `issues` — issue events
- `issue_comment`
- `workflow_run` — CI run completed
- `workflow_job` — job completed (includes `started_at`, `completed_at`, `conclusion`)
- `check_run`, `check_suite`
- `deployment`, `deployment_status`
- `release`
- `milestone`
- `project_v2_item` — project card moves

Webhook payloads mirror the REST response fields above.

## KEY FIELDS BY USE CASE (Quick Reference)

**Cycle time / Lead time:**
- PR `created_at` → `merged_at` (time in review)
- First commit date in branch → PR `merged_at` (full cycle)
- Issue `created_at` → closing commit date (issue resolution time)

**Code churn / PR size:**
- `pr.additions`, `pr.deletions`, `pr.changed_files`, `pr.commits`
- Per-file: diff entry `additions`, `deletions`, `status`

**Review quality:**
- `review.state` distribution per reviewer
- `review_comments` count vs. `reviews` count
- Review thread `isResolved` rate
- Time between `review_requested` (timeline event) and `review.submitted_at`

**CI health:**
- Workflow run `conclusion` rates (success/failure/cancelled)
- `run_duration_ms` trends
- Job `started_at` → `completed_at` per runner
- Step-level timing for bottleneck detection

**Security hygiene:**
- Code scanning `severity` distribution, `fixed_at` presence
- Secret scanning `push_protection_bypassed` counts
- Dependabot `severity`, time-to-fix (`fixed_at` − `created_at`)
- Dependency review `vulnerabilities[]` on each PR diff

**Collaboration patterns:**
- `requested_reviewers` response time (GraphQL timeline)
- `author_association` of commenters
- `PullRequestReviewEvent` frequency per contributor
- Punch card data for work-hour analysis

# Jira REST API: Comprehensive Data & Metrics Reference for Developer Performance Tracking

This reference covers every meaningful data point available from the Jira REST API (both Cloud v3 and Server/Data Center) organized by category with endpoint references and relevance to developer performance tracking.

| Server/DC | `https://{host}/rest/api/2/` | `https://{host}/rest/agile/1.0/` |

## 2. Issue Changelog / History

**Endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}/changelog` (paginated, Cloud)
**Or:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}?expand=changelog` (embeds up to 100 entries)

The changelog is the foundational data source for **time-in-status, cycle time, lead time, and all flow metrics**.

### Changelog Response Structure

```
changelog.histories[] {
  id          — unique change group ID
  author {
    accountId
    displayName
    emailAddress
  }
  created     — ISO-8601 timestamp of the change (USE THIS for time calculations)
  items[] {
    field        — field name (e.g. "status", "assignee", "Story Points")
    fieldtype    — "jira" or "custom"
    fieldId      — field ID (e.g. "status", "customfield_10016")
    from         — previous value ID/key
    fromString   — previous value human-readable
    to           — new value ID/key
    toString     — new value human-readable
  }
}
```

### Fields Tracked in Changelog (everything that changes gets logged)

| Field | fromString / toString examples | Use for |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | "To Do" → "In Progress" → "Done" | **Time in status, cycle time** |
| `assignee` | user display names | Reassignment frequency, handoff count |
| `priority` | "Medium" → "High" | Priority changes during sprint |
| `resolution` | null → "Fixed" | Resolution tracking |
| `Story Points` | "3" → "5" | **Estimation changes** |
| `Sprint` | sprint name changes | Issues moved between sprints (**scope change**) |
| `Fix Version` | version names | Scope changes |
| `Component` | component names | |
| `Assignee` | user names | |
| `labels` | label strings | |
| `parent` | issue keys | Sub-task re-parenting |
| `Link` | link descriptions | Dependency changes |
| `description` | truncated diff | (tracked but content not shown) |
| `summary` | old/new title | Ticket title changes |
| `issuetype` | type names | Issue type changes |
| `Flagged` | "Impediment" ↔ null | Impediment tracking |
| `Rank` | | Backlog reordering |
| `duedate` | date strings | Due date slippage |

**Critical note on initial values:** The changelog does NOT record initial values set at creation. Always combine `issue.created` + `issue.fields.status` at creation time by querying the issue object, then layer on changelog entries to reconstruct state history.

**Sort order:** Items within a history entry have no guaranteed order — always sort `changelog.histories` by `created` timestamp yourself.

### Calculating Time in Status

```
For each issue:
  1. Record (created_at, initial_status) from issue object
  2. Parse changelog for all status changes, sorted by created timestamp
  3. For each consecutive pair of status entries:
     time_in_status = next_change.created - current_change.created
  4. For current/final status:
     time_in_status = now() - last_status_change.created (or resolutiondate)
```

### Cycle Time and Lead Time from Raw Data

| Metric | Calculation |
|---|---|
| Lead time | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| Cycle time (in-progress → done) | Time of first "In Progress" status entry (from changelog) to `resolutiondate` |
| Time in review | Sum of time spent in any "In Review" / "Code Review" / "PR Review" status |
| Time blocked | Sum of time spent in "Blocked" / "On Hold" status; or when `Flagged = Impediment` |
| Time to first response | First comment timestamp - `created` timestamp |

No dedicated "time in status" API endpoint exists natively in Jira Cloud. All such metrics must be derived from changelog data. Third-party marketplace apps (e.g. "Time in Status" by OBSS) offer REST APIs that expose pre-calculated values.

### What Worklogs Enable

- Hours logged per developer per issue

**Pagination:** Worklogs paginate with `startAt` / `maxResults`. The "recently updated" endpoint is the efficient way to sync incrementally — poll `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={lastSyncTimestamp}`, get IDs, then batch-fetch with POST.

## 4. Sprints

**Base:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}`
**Sprints for a board:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint?state=active,future,closed`
**Issues in a sprint:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog`

### Sprint Object Fields

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | int | |
| `self` | URI | |
| `name` | string | |
| `state` | string | `active`, `future`, `closed` |
| `startDate` | ISO-8601 | |
| `endDate` | ISO-8601 | Planned end |
| `completeDate` | ISO-8601 | Actual close date (populated when sprint is closed) |
| `createdDate` | ISO-8601 | |
| `originBoardId` | int | Board the sprint was created on |
| `goal` | string | Sprint goal text |

### Sprint-Level Metrics (derived from issues)

All sprint velocity, burndown, and completion metrics must be computed from issue data — there is no single API endpoint returning pre-calculated velocity.

| Metric | How to compute |
|---|---|
| **Committed points** | Sum of `story_points` for all issues that were in the sprint at start time (use changelog to find which issues were added before sprint start) |
| **Completed points** | Sum of `story_points` for issues with `status.statusCategory = "done"` and `resolutiondate` within sprint dates |
| **Completion rate** | completed / committed |
| **Scope added** | Issues added to sprint after start date (sprint field changed from null/different sprint to this sprint, in changelog, after `startDate`) |
| **Scope removed** | Issues removed mid-sprint (sprint field changed away from this sprint in changelog after `startDate`) |
| **Spillover** | Issues in sprint that did not complete by `endDate` |
| **Velocity** | Average completed points across N sprints |

### Greenhopper/Legacy Velocity Endpoints (Server/DC only, unofficial)

```
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/velocity?rapidViewId={boardId}
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/sprintreport?rapidViewId={boardId}&sprintId={sprintId}
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/scopechangeburndownchart?rapidViewId={boardId}&sprintId={sprintId}
```

These are **undocumented, unsupported, internal APIs** that mirror what Jira's UI charts show. They work on Server/DC but not reliably on Cloud. Atlassian has an open request (JRACLOUD-96617) to provide official reporting APIs. Use them at your own risk and treat them as supplemental, not primary.

**POST (preferred for complex queries):** `POST /rest/api/3/search`

### JQL Fields Reference (all searchable/sortable)

**Identity & Status**
- `project`, `issueType`, `status`, `statusCategory`, `priority`, `resolution`
- `assignee`, `reporter`, `creator`, `voter`, `watcher`
- `component`, `fixVersion`, `affectedVersion`, `labels`, `sprint`

**Dates**
- `created`, `updated`, `resolved`, `due`, `lastViewed`
- `worklogDate`, `worklogAuthor`, `worklogComment`

**Time**
- `originalEstimate`, `remainingEstimate`, `timeSpent`, `workRatio`

**Text**
- `summary`, `description`, `comment`, `text` (searches all text fields)

**Hierarchy**
- `parent`, `issueLink`, `issueLinkType`, `subtasks`

**Relationships**
- `filter` (use a saved filter as part of JQL)
- `sprint`, `sprint in openSprints()`, `sprint in closedSprints()`

**Functions available in JQL**
- `currentUser()`, `membersOf("group")`, `projectsWhereUserHasRole("role")`
- `openSprints()`, `closedSprints()`, `futureSprints()`
- `releasedVersions()`, `unreleasedVersions()`
- `startOfDay()`, `endOfWeek()`, `startOfMonth(-1M)`

### Pagination

| Parameter | Default | Max | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `maxResults` | 50 | 100 (with expand=changelog); up to 5000 with minimal fields | |
| `startAt` | 0 | | Offset-based |
| `total` | — | — | Total matching issues |
| Hard limit | — | 10,000 | Cannot paginate beyond this without cursor-based pagination |

For full dataset export beyond 10k, use `GET /rest/api/3/search/jql` (new cursor-based endpoint) or export via `GET /rest/api/3/issue/archive/export`.

---

## 14. Development Information (Git Integration)

These endpoints surface data from connected tools (Bitbucket, GitHub, GitLab via Jira integrations):

**Official Software API:**
`GET /rest/agile/1.0/issue/{issueIdOrKey}` — returns `development` summary counts

**Internal dev-status API (unofficial, use at risk):**
```
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=repository
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=github&dataType=branch
```

### Data Available per Issue

| DataType | Fields |
|---|---|
| `repository` | Repo name, URL, avatar, branches count, commits count |
| `pullrequest` | PR count, open/merged/declined, PR ID, title, URL, status, reviewer count, last update |
| `branch` | Branch name, URL, last commit (hash, message, author name, author email, timestamp), repo association |

**Note:** This is intended for Jira's internal Development panel UI. It is not officially supported for third-party use and can break across versions. The Jira Software REST API (`/rest/agile/1.0/`) does not officially expose these details in a structured way for Cloud.

---

## 15. Agile Reports — What Exists vs. What Must Be Computed

### What Jira Provides Natively (UI only, no stable public API)

| Report | Available via API? |
|---|---|
| Velocity Chart | No (unofficial GreenHopper endpoint on Server/DC only) |
| Sprint Burndown | No (unofficial GreenHopper endpoint on Server/DC only) |
| Epic Burndown | No |
| Release Burndown | No |
| Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD) | No |
| Control Chart (cycle time scatter) | No |
| Throughput | No |

### What Must Be Computed from Raw API Data

| Metric | Source Data |
|---|---|
| **Velocity** | Sprint issues (`/rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue`), story points field, sprint start/end dates |
| **Burndown** | Sprint issues with story points + changelog status transitions over time |
| **CFD** | Changelog status transitions across date range, bucketed by status |
| **Cycle time** | `resolutiondate` - first "In Progress" timestamp (from changelog) |
| **Lead time** | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| **Throughput** | Count of issues resolved per time period (JQL: `resolved >= -7d`) |
| **Time in status** | Consecutive status changelog entries, diff of `created` timestamps |
| **Scope change** | Sprint field changelog entries after sprint `startDate` |
| **Estimation accuracy** | `workratio` field, or `timeSpentSeconds / originalEstimateSeconds` |
| **PR review time** | Not in Jira — requires Bitbucket/GitHub API |
| **Rework rate** | Issues reopened (status changed from Done category back to active) via changelog |

---

## 16. Useful Derived Metrics and the Raw Data They Require

| Metric | Data Required |
|---|---|
| **Per-developer completed points/sprint** | Sprint issues + story points field + assignee field + sprint dates |
| **Per-developer ticket throughput** | JQL search with assignee + resolved date range |
| **Estimation accuracy by developer** | `workratio` field aggregated by `assignee` |
| **PR description quality proxy** | Comment count on issue + time from creation to first PR (dev-status API) |
| **Ticket aging** | Current date - `created` for unresolved issues (JQL: `status != Done`) |
| **Rework rate** | Count changelog entries where status moved from Done → active category |
| **Sprint predictability** | Committed points (sprint start snapshot) vs. completed (sprint end) — requires changelog |
| **Bug escape rate** | `issuetype = Bug` created after release version `releaseDate` |
| **Blocked time** | Time in status "Blocked" or time with `Flagged = Impediment` from changelog |
| **WIP violations** | Board column WIP limits from board config vs. actual issue counts per status |
| **Issue complexity** | Sub-task count + comment count + worklog entry count + link count |
| **Handoff count** | Count of distinct assignee changes in changelog per issue |
| **Sprint goal attainment** | Sprint `goal` text + whether committed epics/stories completed |

---

## 17. Rate Limits and Practical Constraints

### Cloud

- Rate limiting is points-based per OAuth app/token, not purely request-count
- `GET /rest/api/3/search` with `expand=changelog` can return large payloads — request-time increases significantly
- Changelog via search endpoint: limited to most recent 100 entries per issue; use `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` (paginated) for full history
- Max 10,000 issues accessible via offset pagination; use cursor-based `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` for full exports
- Worklog incremental sync: `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={ms}` is the efficient pattern
- Changelog incremental sync: use `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` with `startAt` pagination

### Server/Data Center

- REST API v2 (not v3)
- GreenHopper internal APIs available for velocity/burndown (undocumented)
- No rate limiting by default, but can be enabled by admins
- Some fields differ: username instead of accountId for users

---

## 18. Key Endpoints Quick Reference

| Purpose | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| Get single issue with full history | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?expand=changelog,names` |
| Search issues with JQL | `POST /rest/api/3/search` with `{"jql":"...","fields":["..."],"expand":["changelog"]}` |
| Issue changelog (paginated) | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` |
| Issue worklogs | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog` |
| Recently updated worklogs (incremental sync) | `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}` |
| Bulk fetch worklogs by ID | `POST /rest/api/3/worklog/list` |
| Issue comments | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment` |
| Issue transitions | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/transitions` |
| Issue links | `GET /rest/api/3/issueLink/{id}` |
| All boards | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board?projectKeyOrId={key}` |
| Board configuration (columns, WIP limits) | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/configuration` |
| Sprints for board | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/sprint?state=closed,active` |
| Sprint details | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}` |
| Issues in sprint | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog` |
| Project versions | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version` |
| Project components | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/component` |
| All fields (discover custom field IDs) | `GET /rest/api/3/field` |
| Users search | `GET /rest/api/3/user/search?query={name}` |
| User groups | `GET /rest/api/3/user/groups?accountId={id}` |
| All statuses | `GET /rest/api/3/status` |
| JQL search (cursor-based, full export) | `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` |
| Dev info for issue (unofficial) | `GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest` |

---

## Key Findings Summary

1. **Jira has no official public API for pre-calculated agile metrics** (velocity, burndown, cycle time, CFD). All must be derived from raw issue + changelog data. Atlassian has an open feature request (JRACLOUD-96617) but it remains unimplemented as of 2026.

2. **The changelog is the single most valuable resource** for developer performance tracking. It records every field change with timestamps — status transitions, assignee changes, story point edits, sprint moves. This is the foundation for time-in-status, cycle time, lead time, scope change, and rework calculations.

3. **Custom field IDs vary by instance.** Always call `GET /rest/api/3/field` to discover the actual IDs for story points, sprint, epic link, etc. in the target instance. Match by `schema.custom` type string (e.g. `com.pyxis.greenhopper.jira:gh-sprint`) rather than by display name, which can be renamed.

4. **The dev-status API** (`/rest/dev-status/1.0/`) surfaces commit, PR, and branch data from connected SCM tools but is an internal, unsupported API — avoid relying on it as a primary data source.

5. **Efficient data ingestion strategy:** Use JQL search for bulk issue retrieval, `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since=` for incremental worklog sync, and paginated `/changelog` endpoint per issue for full history. Store snapshots to avoid re-fetching.

Sources:
- [Jira Cloud Platform REST API v3](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/)
- [Jira Software Cloud REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/)
- [Jira Expressions Type Reference](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/jira-expressions-type-reference/)
- [JQL Fields Reference](https://support.atlassian.com/jira-software-cloud/docs/advanced-search-reference-jql-fields/)
- [How to analyze the history or changelog of an issue](https://support.atlassian.com/jira/kb/how-to-analyze-the-history-or-changelog-of-an-issue-in-jira/)
- [Jira Software Custom Fields (eazyBI)](https://docs.eazybi.com/eazybijira/data-import/data-from-jira-and-apps/jira-software-custom-fields)
- [Jira REST API Examples](https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/jira-rest-api-examples/)
- [Sprint Velocity API discussion](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/public-rest-api-for-sprint-velocity/11391)
- [Public Agile Reporting APIs feature request](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-96617)
- [How to retrieve Pull Request and branch info via REST API](https://support.atlassian.com/jira/kb/how-to-retrieve-pull-request-and-bitbucket-repo-and-branch-information-from-an-issue-using-rest-api/)
- [Jira Issue History - Mike Bowler](https://blog.mikebowler.ca/2024/04/09/jira-issue-history/)
- [Obtaining cycle time via REST API](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/Jira-questions/Obtaing-issue-cycle-time-using-REST-API/qaq-p/1086763)
- [Sprint Burndown via GreenHopper API](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/Jira-questions/How-do-I-fetch-Sprint-Burndown-data-via-API-calls-or-otherwise/qaq-p/2623047)
- [maxResults pagination for Jira Issue Search](https://support.atlassian.com/jira/kb/how-to-use-the-maxresults-api-parameter-for-jira-issue-search-rest-api/)
