content.text:
Now I have comprehensive data from all the key research. Let me compile the full structured reference.

---

# Jira REST API: Comprehensive Data & Metrics Reference for Developer Performance Tracking

This reference covers every meaningful data point available from the Jira REST API (both Cloud v3 and Server/Data Center) organized by category with endpoint references and relevance to developer performance tracking.

---

## API Base URLs

| Environment | Platform API | Agile/Software API |
|---|---|---|
| Cloud | `https://{domain}.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/` | `https://{domain}.atlassian.net/rest/agile/1.0/` |
| Server/DC | `https://{host}/rest/api/2/` | `https://{host}/rest/agile/1.0/` |

---

## 1. Issues

**Primary Endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}`
**Search Endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/search?jql=...&fields=*all&expand=changelog,renderedFields,names,transitions`

### Identity & Metadata

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | Numeric internal ID |
| `key` | string | e.g. "PROJ-123" |
| `self` | URI | Canonical URL |
| `summary` | string | Issue title |
| `description` | ADF document | Rich text (v3) or wiki markup (v2) |
| `environment` | ADF document | Environment description |

### Classification

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `issuetype.id` | string | |
| `issuetype.name` | string | Bug, Story, Task, Epic, Sub-task, etc. |
| `issuetype.subtask` | boolean | |
| `priority.id` | string | |
| `priority.name` | string | Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest |
| `status.id` | string | |
| `status.name` | string | e.g. "In Progress" |
| `status.statusCategory.key` | string | `new`, `indeterminate`, `done` |
| `status.statusCategory.name` | string | To Do, In Progress, Done |
| `resolution.id` | string | |
| `resolution.name` | string | Fixed, Won't Fix, Duplicate, Done, etc. |

### People

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `assignee.accountId` | string | User identifier (Cloud) |
| `assignee.displayName` | string | |
| `assignee.emailAddress` | string | Requires profile visibility |
| `assignee.active` | boolean | |
| `reporter.accountId` | string | |
| `reporter.displayName` | string | |
| `creator.accountId` | string | May differ from reporter |

### Dates & Timestamps

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `created` | ISO-8601 | When issue was created — **lead time start** |
| `updated` | ISO-8601 | Last modification |
| `resolutiondate` | ISO-8601 | When resolved/closed — **lead time end** |
| `duedate` | date | Target completion |
| `lastViewed` | ISO-8601 | |

### Time Tracking (requires time tracking enabled globally)

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `timetracking.originalEstimate` | string | Human-readable, e.g. "2d 4h" |
| `timetracking.originalEstimateSeconds` | int | Raw seconds |
| `timetracking.remainingEstimate` | string | |
| `timetracking.remainingEstimateSeconds` | int | |
| `timetracking.timeSpent` | string | Actual logged time |
| `timetracking.timeSpentSeconds` | int | |
| `aggregatetimeoriginalestimate` | int | Sum including sub-tasks |
| `aggregatetimeestimate` | int | Aggregate remaining |
| `aggregatetimespent` | int | Aggregate logged |
| `timespent` | int | Issue-level only |
| `timeestimate` | int | Current remaining |
| `workratio` | int | (timeSpent / originalEstimate) * 100 — **estimation accuracy** |

### Scope & Hierarchy

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `parent.key` | string | Parent issue (sub-tasks, child issues) |
| `subtasks[]` | array | List of sub-task references |
| `labels[]` | array of strings | Free-form tags |
| `components[].id` | string | |
| `components[].name` | string | |
| `versions[]` | array | "Affects versions" |
| `fixVersions[]` | array | Target release versions |

### Votes & Watchers

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `votes.votes` | int | |
| `watches.watchCount` | int | |

### Agile-Specific Fields (Software API)

| Field | Field ID | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Story Points | `customfield_10016` or `customfield_10028` | Instance-specific; use `GET /rest/api/3/field` to find the ID; schema type `com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.customfieldtypes:float` |
| Sprint | `customfield_10020` | Returns sprint object(s) — current and closed sprints |
| Epic Link | `customfield_10014` | Parent epic key |
| Epic Name | `customfield_10011` | Epic's display name |
| Story Points Estimated | `customfield_10016` | Separate field in team-managed projects |
| Rank | `customfield_10019` | Lexorank string — backlog ordering |
| Flagged | `customfield_10021` | `[{"value": "Impediment"}]` or empty |
| Acceptance Criteria | instance-specific | Common custom field, no standard ID |

**To discover custom field IDs for your instance:**
`GET /rest/api/3/field` — returns all fields with ID, name, schema type

### Attachments

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `attachments[].id` | |
| `attachments[].filename` | |
| `attachments[].author.accountId` | Who attached it |
| `attachments[].created` | When |
| `attachments[].size` | Bytes |
| `attachments[].mimeType` | |

### Issue Links

Returned in `issuelinks[]` array per issue:

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `issuelinks[].id` | |
| `issuelinks[].type.id` | |
| `issuelinks[].type.name` | e.g. "Blocks", "Relates", "Cloners" |
| `issuelinks[].type.inward` | e.g. "is blocked by" |
| `issuelinks[].type.outward` | e.g. "blocks" |
| `issuelinks[].inwardIssue.key` | The linked issue (direction-dependent) |
| `issuelinks[].outwardIssue.key` | |
| `issuelinks[].inwardIssue.status` | Status of linked issue |

**Dedicated endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/issueLink/{linkId}`
**All link types:** `GET /rest/api/3/issueLinkType`

### expand= Options (GET /rest/api/3/issue)

| Value | What it adds |
|---|---|
| `changelog` | Full change history (see Section 2) |
| `renderedFields` | HTML-rendered description/comments |
| `names` | Human-readable field names for custom fields |
| `transitions` | Available status transitions |
| `operations` | User-specific allowed actions |
| `editmeta` | Editable fields for current user |

---

## 2. Issue Changelog / History

**Endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}/changelog` (paginated, Cloud)
**Or:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}?expand=changelog` (embeds up to 100 entries)

The changelog is the foundational data source for **time-in-status, cycle time, lead time, and all flow metrics**.

### Changelog Response Structure

```
changelog.histories[] {
  id          — unique change group ID
  author {
    accountId
    displayName
    emailAddress
  }
  created     — ISO-8601 timestamp of the change (USE THIS for time calculations)
  items[] {
    field        — field name (e.g. "status", "assignee", "Story Points")
    fieldtype    — "jira" or "custom"
    fieldId      — field ID (e.g. "status", "customfield_10016")
    from         — previous value ID/key
    fromString   — previous value human-readable
    to           — new value ID/key
    toString     — new value human-readable
  }
}
```

### Fields Tracked in Changelog (everything that changes gets logged)

| Field | fromString / toString examples | Use for |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | "To Do" → "In Progress" → "Done" | **Time in status, cycle time** |
| `assignee` | user display names | Reassignment frequency, handoff count |
| `priority` | "Medium" → "High" | Priority changes during sprint |
| `resolution` | null → "Fixed" | Resolution tracking |
| `Story Points` | "3" → "5" | **Estimation changes** |
| `Sprint` | sprint name changes | Issues moved between sprints (**scope change**) |
| `Fix Version` | version names | Scope changes |
| `Component` | component names | |
| `Assignee` | user names | |
| `labels` | label strings | |
| `parent` | issue keys | Sub-task re-parenting |
| `Link` | link descriptions | Dependency changes |
| `description` | truncated diff | (tracked but content not shown) |
| `summary` | old/new title | Ticket title changes |
| `issuetype` | type names | Issue type changes |
| `Flagged` | "Impediment" ↔ null | Impediment tracking |
| `Rank` | | Backlog reordering |
| `duedate` | date strings | Due date slippage |

**Critical note on initial values:** The changelog does NOT record initial values set at creation. Always combine `issue.created` + `issue.fields.status` at creation time by querying the issue object, then layer on changelog entries to reconstruct state history.

**Sort order:** Items within a history entry have no guaranteed order — always sort `changelog.histories` by `created` timestamp yourself.

### Calculating Time in Status

```
For each issue:
  1. Record (created_at, initial_status) from issue object
  2. Parse changelog for all status changes, sorted by created timestamp
  3. For each consecutive pair of status entries:
     time_in_status = next_change.created - current_change.created
  4. For current/final status:
     time_in_status = now() - last_status_change.created (or resolutiondate)
```

### Cycle Time and Lead Time from Raw Data

| Metric | Calculation |
|---|---|
| Lead time | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| Cycle time (in-progress → done) | Time of first "In Progress" status entry (from changelog) to `resolutiondate` |
| Time in review | Sum of time spent in any "In Review" / "Code Review" / "PR Review" status |
| Time blocked | Sum of time spent in "Blocked" / "On Hold" status; or when `Flagged = Impediment` |
| Time to first response | First comment timestamp - `created` timestamp |

No dedicated "time in status" API endpoint exists natively in Jira Cloud. All such metrics must be derived from changelog data. Third-party marketplace apps (e.g. "Time in Status" by OBSS) offer REST APIs that expose pre-calculated values.

---

## 3. Worklogs

**Per-issue:** `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{issueIdOrKey}/worklog`
**By ID:** `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/{id}`
**Recently updated worklogs (bulk):** `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={timestamp}` — returns IDs of worklogs updated since epoch timestamp
**Bulk get by IDs:** `POST /rest/api/3/worklog/list`

### Worklog Object Fields

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | |
| `issueId` | string | Parent issue ID |
| `self` | URI | |
| `author.accountId` | string | Who logged the time |
| `author.displayName` | string | |
| `author.active` | boolean | |
| `updateAuthor.accountId` | string | Who last edited the entry |
| `updateAuthor.displayName` | string | |
| `started` | ISO-8601 | When the work was done (user-entered) |
| `timeSpent` | string | Human-readable: "3h 20m" |
| `timeSpentSeconds` | int | Raw value — use this for calculations |
| `comment` | ADF document | Work description |
| `created` | ISO-8601 | When the entry was created in Jira |
| `updated` | ISO-8601 | Last modification |
| `visibility.type` | string | "group" or "role" |
| `visibility.value` | string | Group/role name restriction |

### What Worklogs Enable

- Hours logged per developer per issue
- Hours logged per developer per sprint (join via sprint custom field on issue)
- Actual vs. estimated time per story/epic
- Developer capacity utilization over time periods
- Billing/chargeback by component or epic

**Pagination:** Worklogs paginate with `startAt` / `maxResults`. The "recently updated" endpoint is the efficient way to sync incrementally — poll `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={lastSyncTimestamp}`, get IDs, then batch-fetch with POST.

---

## 4. Sprints

**Base:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}`
**Sprints for a board:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint?state=active,future,closed`
**Issues in a sprint:** `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{sprintId}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog`

### Sprint Object Fields

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | int | |
| `self` | URI | |
| `name` | string | |
| `state` | string | `active`, `future`, `closed` |
| `startDate` | ISO-8601 | |
| `endDate` | ISO-8601 | Planned end |
| `completeDate` | ISO-8601 | Actual close date (populated when sprint is closed) |
| `createdDate` | ISO-8601 | |
| `originBoardId` | int | Board the sprint was created on |
| `goal` | string | Sprint goal text |

### Sprint-Level Metrics (derived from issues)

All sprint velocity, burndown, and completion metrics must be computed from issue data — there is no single API endpoint returning pre-calculated velocity.

| Metric | How to compute |
|---|---|
| **Committed points** | Sum of `story_points` for all issues that were in the sprint at start time (use changelog to find which issues were added before sprint start) |
| **Completed points** | Sum of `story_points` for issues with `status.statusCategory = "done"` and `resolutiondate` within sprint dates |
| **Completion rate** | completed / committed |
| **Scope added** | Issues added to sprint after start date (sprint field changed from null/different sprint to this sprint, in changelog, after `startDate`) |
| **Scope removed** | Issues removed mid-sprint (sprint field changed away from this sprint in changelog after `startDate`) |
| **Spillover** | Issues in sprint that did not complete by `endDate` |
| **Velocity** | Average completed points across N sprints |

### Greenhopper/Legacy Velocity Endpoints (Server/DC only, unofficial)

```
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/velocity?rapidViewId={boardId}
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/sprintreport?rapidViewId={boardId}&sprintId={sprintId}
GET /rest/greenhopper/1.0/rapid/charts/scopechangeburndownchart?rapidViewId={boardId}&sprintId={sprintId}
```

These are **undocumented, unsupported, internal APIs** that mirror what Jira's UI charts show. They work on Server/DC but not reliably on Cloud. Atlassian has an open request (JRACLOUD-96617) to provide official reporting APIs. Use them at your own risk and treat them as supplemental, not primary.

## 13. Issue Search (JQL)

**Endpoint:** `GET /rest/api/3/search?jql=...`

### JQL Fields Reference (all searchable/sortable)

**Identity & Status**
- `project`, `issueType`, `status`, `statusCategory`, `priority`, `resolution`
- `assignee`, `reporter`, `creator`, `voter`, `watcher`
- `component`, `fixVersion`, `affectedVersion`, `labels`, `sprint`

**Dates**
- `created`, `updated`, `resolved`, `due`, `lastViewed`
- `worklogDate`, `worklogAuthor`, `worklogComment`

**Time**
- `originalEstimate`, `remainingEstimate`, `timeSpent`, `workRatio`

**Text**
- `summary`, `description`, `comment`, `text` (searches all text fields)

**Hierarchy**
- `parent`, `issueLink`, `issueLinkType`, `subtasks`

**Relationships**
- `filter` (use a saved filter as part of JQL)
- `sprint`, `sprint in openSprints()`, `sprint in closedSprints()`

**Functions available in JQL**
- `currentUser()`, `membersOf("group")`, `projectsWhereUserHasRole("role")`
- `openSprints()`, `closedSprints()`, `futureSprints()`
- `releasedVersions()`, `unreleasedVersions()`
- `startOfDay()`, `endOfWeek()`, `startOfMonth(-1M)`

### Pagination

| Parameter | Default | Max | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `maxResults` | 50 | 100 (with expand=changelog); up to 5000 with minimal fields | |
| `startAt` | 0 | | Offset-based |
| `total` | — | — | Total matching issues |
| Hard limit | — | 10,000 | Cannot paginate beyond this without cursor-based pagination |

For full dataset export beyond 10k, use `GET /rest/api/3/search/jql` (new cursor-based endpoint) or export via `GET /rest/api/3/issue/archive/export`.

---

## 14. Development Information (Git Integration)

These endpoints surface data from connected tools (Bitbucket, GitHub, GitLab via Jira integrations):

**Official Software API:**
`GET /rest/agile/1.0/issue/{issueIdOrKey}` — returns `development` summary counts

**Internal dev-status API (unofficial, use at risk):**
```
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=repository
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=github&dataType=branch
```

### Data Available per Issue

| DataType | Fields |
|---|---|
| `repository` | Repo name, URL, avatar, branches count, commits count |
| `pullrequest` | PR count, open/merged/declined, PR ID, title, URL, status, reviewer count, last update |
| `branch` | Branch name, URL, last commit (hash, message, author name, author email, timestamp), repo association |

**Note:** This is intended for Jira's internal Development panel UI. It is not officially supported for third-party use and can break across versions. The Jira Software REST API (`/rest/agile/1.0/`) does not officially expose these details in a structured way for Cloud.

---

## 15. Agile Reports — What Exists vs. What Must Be Computed

### What Jira Provides Natively (UI only, no stable public API)

| Report | Available via API? |
|---|---|
| Velocity Chart | No (unofficial GreenHopper endpoint on Server/DC only) |
| Sprint Burndown | No (unofficial GreenHopper endpoint on Server/DC only) |
| Epic Burndown | No |
| Release Burndown | No |
| Cumulative Flow Diagram (CFD) | No |
| Control Chart (cycle time scatter) | No |
| Throughput | No |

### What Must Be Computed from Raw API Data

| Metric | Source Data |
|---|---|
| **Velocity** | Sprint issues (`/rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue`), story points field, sprint start/end dates |
| **Burndown** | Sprint issues with story points + changelog status transitions over time |
| **CFD** | Changelog status transitions across date range, bucketed by status |
| **Cycle time** | `resolutiondate` - first "In Progress" timestamp (from changelog) |
| **Lead time** | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| **Throughput** | Count of issues resolved per time period (JQL: `resolved >= -7d`) |
| **Time in status** | Consecutive status changelog entries, diff of `created` timestamps |
| **Scope change** | Sprint field changelog entries after sprint `startDate` |
| **Estimation accuracy** | `workratio` field, or `timeSpentSeconds / originalEstimateSeconds` |
| **PR review time** | Not in Jira — requires Bitbucket/GitHub API |
| **Rework rate** | Issues reopened (status changed from Done category back to active) via changelog |

---

## 16. Useful Derived Metrics and the Raw Data They Require

| Metric | Data Required |
|---|---|
| **Per-developer completed points/sprint** | Sprint issues + story points field + assignee field + sprint dates |
| **Per-developer ticket throughput** | JQL search with assignee + resolved date range |
| **Estimation accuracy by developer** | `workratio` field aggregated by `assignee` |
| **PR description quality proxy** | Comment count on issue + time from creation to first PR (dev-status API) |
| **Ticket aging** | Current date - `created` for unresolved issues (JQL: `status != Done`) |
| **Rework rate** | Count changelog entries where status moved from Done → active category |
| **Sprint predictability** | Committed points (sprint start snapshot) vs. completed (sprint end) — requires changelog |
| **Bug escape rate** | `issuetype = Bug` created after release version `releaseDate` |
| **Blocked time** | Time in status "Blocked" or time with `Flagged = Impediment` from changelog |
| **WIP violations** | Board column WIP limits from board config vs. actual issue counts per status |
| **Issue complexity** | Sub-task count + comment count + worklog entry count + link count |
| **Handoff count** | Count of distinct assignee changes in changelog per issue |
| **Sprint goal attainment** | Sprint `goal` text + whether committed epics/stories completed |

---

## 17. Rate Limits and Practical Constraints

### Cloud

- Rate limiting is points-based per OAuth app/token, not purely request-count
- `GET /rest/api/3/search` with `expand=changelog` can return large payloads — request-time increases significantly
- Changelog via search endpoint: limited to most recent 100 entries per issue; use `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` (paginated) for full history
- Max 10,000 issues accessible via offset pagination; use cursor-based `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` for full exports
- Worklog incremental sync: `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={ms}` is the efficient pattern
- Changelog incremental sync: use `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` with `startAt` pagination

### Server/Data Center

- REST API v2 (not v3)
- GreenHopper internal APIs available for velocity/burndown (undocumented)
- No rate limiting by default, but can be enabled by admins
- Some fields differ: username instead of accountId for users

---

## 18. Key Endpoints Quick Reference

| Purpose | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| Get single issue with full history | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?expand=changelog,names` |
| Search issues with JQL | `POST /rest/api/3/search` with `{"jql":"...","fields":["..."],"expand":["changelog"]}` |
| Issue changelog (paginated) | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` |
| Issue worklogs | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog` |
| Recently updated worklogs (incremental sync) | `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}` |
| Bulk fetch worklogs by ID | `POST /rest/api/3/worklog/list` |
| Issue comments | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment` |
| Issue transitions | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/transitions` |
| Issue links | `GET /rest/api/3/issueLink/{id}` |
| All boards | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board?projectKeyOrId={key}` |
| Board configuration (columns, WIP limits) | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/configuration` |
| Sprints for board | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/sprint?state=closed,active` |
| Sprint details | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}` |
| Issues in sprint | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog` |
| Project versions | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version` |
| Project components | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/component` |
| All fields (discover custom field IDs) | `GET /rest/api/3/field` |
| Users search | `GET /rest/api/3/user/search?query={name}` |
| User groups | `GET /rest/api/3/user/groups?accountId={id}` |
| All statuses | `GET /rest/api/3/status` |
| JQL search (cursor-based, full export) | `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` |
| Dev info for issue (unofficial) | `GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest` |

---

## Key Findings Summary

1. **Jira has no official public API for pre-calculated agile metrics** (velocity, burndown, cycle time, CFD). All must be derived from raw issue + changelog data. Atlassian has an open feature request (JRACLOUD-96617) but it remains unimplemented as of 2026.

2. **The changelog is the single most valuable resource** for developer performance tracking. It records every field change with timestamps — status transitions, assignee changes, story point edits, sprint moves. This is the foundation for time-in-status, cycle time, lead time, scope change, and rework calculations.

3. **Custom field IDs vary by instance.** Always call `GET /rest/api/3/field` to discover the actual IDs for story points, sprint, epic link, etc. in the target instance. Match by `schema.custom` type string (e.g. `com.pyxis.greenhopper.jira:gh-sprint`) rather than by display name, which can be renamed.

4. **The dev-status API** (`/rest/dev-status/1.0/`) surfaces commit, PR, and branch data from connected SCM tools but is an internal, unsupported API — avoid relying on it as a primary data source.

5. **Efficient data ingestion strategy:** Use JQL search for bulk issue retrieval, `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since=` for incremental worklog sync, and paginated `/changelog` endpoint per issue for full history. Store snapshots to avoid re-fetching.

Sources:
- [Jira Cloud Platform REST API v3](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/)
- [Jira Software Cloud REST API](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/rest/)
- [Jira Expressions Type Reference](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/jira-expressions-type-reference/)
- [JQL Fields Reference](https://support.atlassian.com/jira-software-cloud/docs/advanced-search-reference-jql-fields/)
- [How to analyze the history or changelog of an issue](https://support.atlassian.com/jira/kb/how-to-analyze-the-history-or-changelog-of-an-issue-in-jira/)
- [Jira Software Custom Fields (eazyBI)](https://docs.eazybi.com/eazybijira/data-import/data-from-jira-and-apps/jira-software-custom-fields)
- [Jira REST API Examples](https://developer.atlassian.com/server/jira/platform/jira-rest-api-examples/)
- [Sprint Velocity API discussion](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/public-rest-api-for-sprint-velocity/11391)
- [Public Agile Reporting APIs feature request](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-96617)
- [How to retrieve Pull Request and branch info via REST API](https://support.atlassian.com/jira/kb/how-to-retrieve-pull-request-and-bitbucket-repo-and-branch-information-from-an-issue-using-rest-api/)
- [Jira Issue History - Mike Bowler](https://blog.mikebowler.ca/2024/04/09/jira-issue-history/)
- [Obtaining cycle time via REST API](https://community.atlassian.com/forums/Jira-questions/Obtaing-issue-cycle-time-using-REST-API/qaq-p/1086763)

# GitHub API — Available Data

Full reference of data extractable from GitHub REST + GraphQL APIs for developer performance tracking.

---

## Commits

### Per Commit (single-commit endpoint only)
- `sha`, `message` (subject + body), `authored_at`, `committed_at`
- `author` + `committer` (may differ on rebase/cherry-pick)
- `stats.additions`, `stats.deletions`, `stats.total`
- `parents[]` — count > 1 = merge commit
- `verification.verified` — GPG/SSH signed
- `comment_count`
- Per file: `filename`, `status` (added/removed/modified/renamed/copied), `additions`, `deletions`, `patch`

### Aggregate Stats (cached, repo-level)
- **`/stats/contributors`** — per contributor per week: `additions`, `deletions`, `commits` (52 weeks)
- **`/stats/commit_activity`** — per week total + per day of week (52 weeks)
- **`/stats/code_frequency`** — weekly additions/deletions since repo creation
- **`/stats/participation`** — last 52 weeks: all vs. owner commits
- **`/stats/punch_card`** — hourly distribution by day of week — work pattern analysis

> **Note**: `additions`/`deletions` require N+1 calls on list endpoints. Use stats endpoints for aggregates.

---

## Pull Requests

### Core Fields
- `number`, `title`, `body`, `state` (open/closed), `draft`, `merged`, `locked`
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `merged_at` — **cycle time foundation**
- `additions`, `deletions`, `changed_files`, `commits` — PR size
- `review_comments` — inline review comments count
- `comments` — general comments count
- `merge_commit_sha`, `merged_by`
- `merge_method` — `merge`, `squash`, `rebase`
- `auto_merge` — enabled_by, method, commit config
- `author_association` — OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR/CONTRIBUTOR/FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR/FIRST_TIMER
- `requested_reviewers[]`, `requested_teams[]`
- `assignees[]`, `labels[]`, `milestone`
- `mergeable`, `mergeable_state`, `rebaseable`
- `maintainer_can_modify`

### GraphQL-Only PR Fields
- `reviewDecision` — APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / REVIEW_REQUIRED
- `reviewThreads` — each thread: `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, `path`, `line`, `resolvedBy`
- `timelineItems` — full ordered event stream (labels, assignments, reviews, commits, status changes)
- `closingIssuesReferences` — issues that close on merge
- `participants` — all users who touched the PR
- `suggestedReviewers` — ML-recommended reviewers
- `isInMergeQueue`, `isCrossRepository` (fork PR)
- `totalCommentsCount` — combined count

---

## Code Reviews

### Review Object
- `state` — APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / COMMENTED / PENDING / DISMISSED
- `submitted_at` — **time-to-review foundation**
- `reviewer` (user), `author_association`
- `commit_id` — which commit was reviewed

### Review Comments (Inline)
- `path`, `line`, `original_line`, `side` (LEFT/RIGHT)
- `diff_hunk` — surrounding code context
- `body`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `in_reply_to_id` — thread structure
- `reactions` — 👍 👎 😄 😕 ❤️ 🎉 👀 🚀
- `resolved` (boolean)

### GraphQL Review Thread Detail
- `isResolved`, `resolvedBy`, `isOutdated`, `isCollapsed`
- `path`, `line`, `startLine`, `diffSide`

---

## Issues

### Core Fields
- `number`, `title`, `body`, `state` (open/closed)
- `state_reason` — completed / reopened / not_planned / duplicate
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `closed_at`, `closed_by`
- `assignees[]`, `labels[]`, `milestone`
- `comments` count
- `reactions` — full rollup
- `author_association`
- `parent_issue_url`, `sub_issues_summary` (total/completed/percent)
- `issue_dependencies_summary` (blocked_by/blocking counts)
- `pull_request.merged_at` — present if issue linked to a PR

### Timeline Events (per issue)
All state changes tracked: labeled/unlabeled, assigned/unassigned, milestoned, renamed, closed/reopened, locked, referenced, cross-referenced, review_requested, reviewed, committed, state_change, added_to_project, moved_column_in_project

---

## Repository Statistics & Traffic

### Contributor Stats (`/stats/contributors`)
Per contributor, per week for 52 weeks: `additions`, `deletions`, `commits`

### Traffic (14-day window, requires push access)
- `/traffic/views` — total + unique views per day
- `/traffic/clones` — total + unique clones per day
- `/traffic/popular/referrers` — top 10 referrers
- `/traffic/popular/paths` — top 10 popular paths

### Repository Fields
- `size`, `stargazers_count`, `forks_count`, `watchers_count`, `open_issues_count`
- `language` (primary), `topics[]`
- `allow_merge_commit`, `allow_squash_merge`, `allow_rebase_merge`, `allow_auto_merge`, `delete_branch_on_merge`
- `security_and_analysis` — advanced security, secret scanning status
- `web_commit_signoff_required`
- Languages breakdown: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages` → bytes per language

---

## GitHub Actions / CI

### Workflow Runs
- `status` — queued/in_progress/completed/waiting/requested/pending
- `conclusion` — success/failure/cancelled/neutral/skipped/timed_out/action_required
- `event` — push/pull_request/schedule/workflow_dispatch/release/etc.
- `created_at`, `run_started_at`, `updated_at` — queue time = `run_started_at - created_at`
- `actor`, `triggering_actor`
- `head_sha`, `head_branch`, `workflow_id`
- `/timing` endpoint: `run_duration_ms`, `billable.{OS}.total_ms` per job

### Workflow Jobs
- `name`, `status`, `conclusion`
- `created_at`, `started_at`, `completed_at` — job duration
- `runner_name`, `runner_group_name`, `labels[]`
- `steps[]` — each step: `name`, `status`, `conclusion`, `started_at`, `completed_at`

### Check Runs (CI integrations — Codecov, SonarQube, etc.)
- `name`, `app.name` — which tool
- `status`, `conclusion`
- `started_at`, `completed_at`
- `output.annotations_count` — issues found
- `output.title`, `.summary`

### Commit Statuses (legacy CI)
- `state` — error/failure/pending/success
- `context` — CI system name
- `creator`, `created_at`

---

## Deployments & Environments

### Deployments
- `sha`, `ref`, `environment`, `task` (deploy/deploy:migrations)
- `creator`, `created_at`
- `transient_environment`, `production_environment`

### Deployment Statuses
- `state` — error/failure/pending/in_progress/queued/success
- `environment_url`, `log_url`
- `creator`, `created_at`

### Environments
- `name`, `protection_rules[]` (required reviewers, wait timers)
- `deployment_branch_policy`

---

## Security Signals

### Code Scanning Alerts
- `state` — open/dismissed/fixed
- `severity` — critical/high/medium/low/warning/note/error
- `rule.id`, `rule.name`, `rule.security_severity_level`, `rule.tags[]`
- `tool.name`, `tool.version` (CodeQL, Semgrep, etc.)
- `created_at`, `fixed_at`, `dismissed_at`
- `dismissed_reason` — false positive / won't fix / used in tests
- `dismissed_by`, `dismissal_approved_by`
- `most_recent_instance.location` — file path + line/column
- `most_recent_instance.classifications` — source/generated/test/library

### Secret Scanning Alerts
- `secret_type`, `provider`, `validity` — active/inactive/unknown
- `push_protection_bypassed` — boolean, **developer bypassed protection**
- `push_protection_bypassed_by`, `push_protection_bypassed_at`
- `resolution` — false_positive/wont_fix/revoked/used_in_tests
- `publicly_leaked`, `multi_repo`

### Dependabot Alerts
- `severity` — low/medium/high/critical
- `package.ecosystem`, `package.name`, `vulnerable_version_range`
- `first_patched_version`
- `dismissed_reason` — fix_started/inaccurate/no_bandwidth/not_used/tolerable_risk
- `dependency.scope` — development/runtime
- `auto_dismissed_at`, `fixed_at`

### Dependency Review (per PR)
Per changed dependency: `change_type` (added/removed), `ecosystem`, `name`, `version`, `vulnerabilities[]` with severity + GHSA/CVE IDs

---

## Users / Contributors

### REST Profile
- `login`, `name`, `company`, `location`, `email`, `bio`, `twitter_username`
- `public_repos`, `public_gists`, `followers`, `following`
- `created_at` — account age (seniority signal)

### GraphQL Contribution Data (`contributionsCollection`)
Time-bounded (specify `from`/`to`):
- `totalCommitContributions`
- `totalPullRequestContributions`
- `totalIssueContributions`
- `totalPullRequestReviewContributions`
- `totalRepositoriesWithContributedCommits/Issues/PRs/Reviews`
- `restrictedContributionsCount` — private repo contributions (count only, no detail)
- Per-repo breakdown: `commitContributionsByRepository`, `pullRequestContributionsByRepository`, etc.
- `contributionCalendar` — full 52-week heatmap grid

### Per-day Contribution Detail (GraphQL)
`CreatedCommitContribution`: `commitCount`, `occurredAt`, `repository`

---

## Releases
- `tag_name`, `name`, `draft`, `prerelease`
- `created_at`, `published_at`
- `author`
- `assets[]` — `name`, `size`, `download_count`, `content_type`, `created_at`, `expires_at`
- `reactions`

---

## Projects v2 (GraphQL)
- `ProjectV2Item` — each card: `content` (Issue/PR), `fieldValues` (custom fields)
- Custom field types: text, number, date, single-select, iteration, assignees, labels, milestone
- Status updates with history: `status`, `bodyText`, `createdAt`, `creator`
- `ProjectV2View` configurations

---

## Discussions (GraphQL Only)
- `title`, `category.name`, `category.isAnswerable`
- `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `closedAt`
- `comments.totalCount`
- `answerChosenAt`, `answerChosenBy` — Q&A resolution
- `upvoteCount`, `stateReason` (DUPLICATE/OUTDATED/RESOLVED)

---

## Webhooks (Real-Time Events)

Subscribe instead of polling. Key events for performance tracking:
- `push` — commits with file changes
- `pull_request` — all lifecycle (opened/closed/merged/draft/ready_for_review/review_requested)
- `pull_request_review`, `pull_request_review_comment`, `pull_request_review_thread`
- `issues`, `issue_comment`
- `workflow_run`, `workflow_job` — CI completion with timing
- `check_run`, `check_suite`
- `deployment`, `deployment_status`
- `release`
- `project_v2_item` — card moved in project board

---

## Rate Limits

| Auth Type | REST/hr | GraphQL points/hr |
|-----------|---------|-------------------|
| GitHub App installation | 5,000–12,500 (scales) | 5,000 |
| Personal access token | 5,000 | 5,000 |
| Enterprise Cloud | 15,000 | 10,000 |

**Key rules:**
- 100 concurrent requests max
- 900 REST points/minute
- Stats endpoints: async computed, return 202 until ready — cache aggressively
- `If-None-Match` / `If-Modified-Since` — 304 responses don't count against limit
- Commit stats (additions/deletions) need one call per commit — batch via GraphQL

---

## GraphQL-Only Features (No REST Equivalent)

| Feature | Notes |
|---------|-------|
| Contribution heatmap | `user.contributionsCollection` — full 52-week grid |
| PR review decision | `APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / REVIEW_REQUIRED` |
| PR timeline items | Full ordered event stream |
| PR review thread resolution | `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, `resolvedBy` |
| PR closing issues | Issues auto-closed on merge |
| Discussions | Entire feature |
| Merge queue position | `isInMergeQueue` |
| Branch protection rules | Required reviews, status checks, restrictions |
| Projects v2 full data | Field values, iterations, custom fields |
| User status | Current availability message |

toolUseResult.content:
# GitHub API — Available Data

Full reference of data extractable from GitHub REST + GraphQL APIs for developer performance tracking.

---

## Commits

### Per Commit (single-commit endpoint only)
- `sha`, `message` (subject + body), `authored_at`, `committed_at`
- `author` + `committer` (may differ on rebase/cherry-pick)
- `stats.additions`, `stats.deletions`, `stats.total`
- `parents[]` — count > 1 = merge commit
- `verification.verified` — GPG/SSH signed
- `comment_count`
- Per file: `filename`, `status` (added/removed/modified/renamed/copied), `additions`, `deletions`, `patch`

### Aggregate Stats (cached, repo-level)
- **`/stats/contributors`** — per contributor per week: `additions`, `deletions`, `commits` (52 weeks)
- **`/stats/commit_activity`** — per week total + per day of week (52 weeks)
- **`/stats/code_frequency`** — weekly additions/deletions since repo creation
- **`/stats/participation`** — last 52 weeks: all vs. owner commits
- **`/stats/punch_card`** — hourly distribution by day of week — work pattern analysis

> **Note**: `additions`/`deletions` require N+1 calls on list endpoints. Use stats endpoints for aggregates.

---

- `merge_commit_sha`, `merged_by`

- `auto_merge` — enabled_by, method, commit config
- `author_association` — OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR/CONTRIBUTOR/FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR/FIRST_TIMER

- `assignees[]`, `labels[]`, `milestone`

- `timelineItems` — full ordered event stream (labels, assignments, reviews, commits, status changes)
- `closingIssuesReferences` — issues that close on merge
- `participants` — all users who touched the PR

input.content:
# Jira API — Available Data

Full reference of data extractable from Jira REST API (Cloud v3 + Server/DC) for developer performance tracking.

---

## Issues

### Identity & Classification
- `id`, `key` (e.g. PROJ-123), `summary`
- `issuetype.name` — Bug/Story/Task/Epic/Sub-task
- `priority.name` — Highest/High/Medium/Low/Lowest
- `status.name`, `status.statusCategory.key` — `new` / `indeterminate` / `done`
- `resolution.name` — Fixed/Won't Fix/Duplicate/Done
- `labels[]`, `components[]`, `versions[]` (affects), `fixVersions[]`

### People
- `assignee.accountId`, `assignee.displayName`, `assignee.emailAddress`
- `reporter.accountId`, `reporter.displayName`
- `creator.accountId` — may differ from reporter

### Timestamps
- `created` — **lead time start**
- `updated` — last activity
- `resolutiondate` — **lead time end**
- `duedate` — target completion

### Time Tracking (requires global setting)
- `timetracking.originalEstimateSeconds` — planned hours
- `timetracking.timeSpentSeconds` — actual logged time
- `timetracking.remainingEstimateSeconds`
- `aggregatetimeoriginalestimate` — sum including sub-tasks
- `aggregatetimespent` — aggregate logged
- `workratio` — `(timeSpent / originalEstimate) * 100` — **estimation accuracy**

### Hierarchy & Links
- `parent.key` — parent issue/epic
- `subtasks[]` — sub-task references
- `issuelinks[]` — each with:
  - `type.name` — Blocks/Relates/Cloners/Duplicate
  - `type.inward` / `type.outward` — directional labels
  - `inwardIssue.key` / `outwardIssue.key`
  - `inwardIssue.status` — status of linked issue

### Agile Fields (custom field IDs vary per instance)
- Story Points — `customfield_10016` or `customfield_10028` (discover via `GET /rest/api/3/field`)
- Sprint — `customfield_10020` — returns sprint object(s) with id, name, state, startDate, endDate
- Epic Link — `customfield_10014` — parent epic key
- Epic Name — `customfield_10011`
- Rank — `customfield_10019` — Lexorank string
- Flagged — `customfield_10021` — `[{"value": "Impediment"}]` or empty

> Always call `GET /rest/api/3/field` to discover custom field IDs for a given instance. Match by `schema.custom` type, not display name.

### Votes, Watches, Attachments
- `votes.votes`, `watches.watchCount`
- `attachments[]` — filename, author, size, mimeType, created

---

## Changelog (Most Valuable Data Source)

`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` (paginated) or `expand=changelog` on issue fetch.

The changelog records **every field change with timestamps**. Foundation for all flow metrics.

### Structure
```
histories[] {
  id
  author { accountId, displayName, emailAddress }
  created    ← ISO-8601 timestamp of change
  items[] {
    field        ← field name
    fieldId      ← field ID
    from / fromString  ← previous value
    to / toString      ← new value
  }
}
```

### Fields Tracked in Changelog

| Field | Use For |
|-------|---------|
| `status` | **Time in status, cycle time** |
| `assignee` | Handoff count, reassignment frequency |
| `priority` | Priority escalations during sprint |
| `Story Points` | Estimation changes (re-pointing) |
| `Sprint` | Issues moved between sprints — **scope change** |
| `Fix Version` | Release scope changes |
| `Flagged` | Impediment/blocker tracking |
| `resolution` | Resolution date reconstruction |
| `summary` | Title rewrites (ticket quality signal) |
| `issuetype` | Type upgrades (bug discovered mid-story) |
| `duedate` | Due date slippage |
| `parent` | Sub-task re-parenting |

> **Critical**: Changelog does NOT record initial values. Combine `issue.created` + initial `issue.fields.status` with changelog to reconstruct full state history.

### Calculating Time in Status
```
1. Record (created_at, initial_status) from issue object
2. Sort changelog.histories by created timestamp
3. For each status change: time_in_status = next_change.created - current_change.created
4. Current status duration = now() - last_status_change.created
```

### Derived Flow Metrics from Changelog

| Metric | Calculation |
|--------|-------------|
| Lead time | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| Cycle time | First "In Progress" timestamp (changelog) → `resolutiondate` |
| Time in code review | Sum of time in "In Review" / "Code Review" status |
| Blocked time | Sum of time in "Blocked" / "On Hold" OR when `Flagged = Impediment` |
| Rework rate | Status moved from Done category → active category (reopens) |
| Handoff count | Distinct assignee changes in changelog |
| Scope change | Sprint field changes after sprint `startDate` |
| Estimation changes | Story point changes in changelog |
| Re-points frequency | Count of `Story Points` changelog entries |

---

## Worklogs

`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog`

### Fields
- `author.accountId`, `author.displayName`
- `started` — when work was done (user-entered)
- `timeSpentSeconds` — use this for calculations
- `comment` — work description
- `created`, `updated`

### Incremental Sync Pattern
```
GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}  → IDs of updated worklogs
POST /rest/api/3/worklog/list                     → bulk fetch by IDs
```

### What Worklogs Enable
- Hours logged per developer per issue/sprint/epic
- Actual vs. estimated time by developer
- Developer capacity utilization over time
- After-hours logging detection (via `started` timestamp)

---

## Sprints

`GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint?state=active,future,closed`

### Sprint Fields
- `id`, `name`, `state` — active/future/closed
- `startDate`, `endDate` — planned
- `completeDate` — actual close (populated when sprint closed)
- `goal` — sprint goal text
- `originBoardId`

### Sprint-Level Metrics (all derived from issue data — no native API)

| Metric | How to Compute |
|--------|----------------|
| Committed points | Story points for issues in sprint at `startDate` (use changelog to reconstruct) |
| Completed points | Story points for issues with status.category = done by sprint `endDate` |
| Completion rate | `completed / committed` |
| Scope added | Issues where sprint field changed TO this sprint in changelog after `startDate` |
| Scope removed | Issues where sprint field changed AWAY in changelog after `startDate` |
| Spillover | Issues in sprint, not completed by `endDate` |
| Velocity | Average completed points across last N sprints |

> **No official API for velocity/burndown/CFD on Jira Cloud.** Everything must be derived from raw issue + changelog data. Atlassian ticket JRACLOUD-96617 remains open.

---

## Boards

`GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/configuration`

### Board Config
- `type` — scrum/kanban
- `columnConfig.columns[]` — each column:
  - `name` — column display name
  - `statuses[]` — workflow statuses mapped to this column
  - `min` / `max` — WIP limits
- `estimation.field.fieldId` — story_points or originalEstimate
- `filter.id` — JQL filter defining board scope
- `ranking.rankCustomFieldId`

### WIP Violations
Board config `max` limits + actual issue counts per status column = WIP violation detection.

---

## Projects

- `key`, `name`, `projectTypeKey` — software/business/service_desk
- `simplified` — `true` = team-managed (next-gen), `false` = classic
- `lead.accountId`, `lead.displayName`
- `components[]`, `issueTypes[]`, `versions[]`
- `insight.totalIssueCount`, `insight.lastIssueUpdateTime`
- `projectCategory.name`

---

## Versions / Releases

`GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version`

- `name`, `description`
- `released` (boolean), `archived`, `overdue`
- `startDate`, `releaseDate`
- `issuesFixedCount`, `issuesAffectedCount` (from `/relatedIssueCounts` endpoint)

---

## Components

`GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/component`

- `name`, `description`
- `lead.accountId` — component owner
- `assigneeType` — PROJECT_DEFAULT/COMPONENT_LEAD/PROJECT_LEAD/UNASSIGNED
- `issueCount`

---

## Users

`GET /rest/api/3/user?accountId={id}`

- `accountId`, `displayName`, `emailAddress`
- `accountType` — atlassian/app/customer
- `active` (boolean)
- `timeZone` — IANA timezone (work hours analysis)
- `locale`

---

## Comments

`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment`

- `author.accountId`, `author.displayName`
- `body` — rich text (ADF v3)
- `created`, `updated`
- `jsdPublic` — Service Desk: visible to customer?

### What Comments Enable
- First response time: `first_comment.created - issue.created`
- Discussion depth per ticket
- Non-assignee participant identification

---

## JQL Search

`POST /rest/api/3/search`

### Useful JQL Patterns

```jql
-- Developer output in sprint
assignee = "accountId" AND sprint in closedSprints() AND status = Done

-- Cycle time data
project = PROJ AND status changed to "Done" DURING ("2024-01-01", "2024-12-31")

-- Bugs introduced vs. bugs fixed
issuetype = Bug AND creator = "accountId" AND created >= -30d
issuetype = Bug AND assignee = "accountId" AND resolutiondate >= -30d

-- Re-opened issues (rework)
status changed to "In Progress" after resolutiondate is not EMPTY

-- Blockers
labels = "blocked" OR flagged = "Impediment"

-- Scope creep
sprint changed DURING (sprintStart, sprintEnd) AND sprint = currentSprint()
```

### Functions
- `currentUser()`, `membersOf("group")`
- `openSprints()`, `closedSprints()`, `futureSprints()`
- `startOfDay()`, `startOfWeek(-1w)`, `startOfMonth(-1M)`

### Pagination Limits
- Max 100 results per page with `expand=changelog`
- Max 10,000 offset — use `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` (cursor-based) for full export

---

## Development Information (Unofficial)

Links to connected SCM tools (GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab):

```
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=github&dataType=branch
```

Returns: PR count, open/merged/declined, PR title/URL, branch name, last commit info.

> ⚠️ Internal/unofficial API — not supported, may break. Use as supplemental only. Primary Git data should come from GitHub API directly.

---

## Key Endpoints Quick Reference

| Purpose | Endpoint |
|---------|----------|
| Issue with full history | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?expand=changelog,names` |
| Bulk issue search | `POST /rest/api/3/search` |
| Full changelog (paginated) | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` |
| Worklogs | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog` |
| Incremental worklog sync | `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}` |
| All custom fields | `GET /rest/api/3/field` |
| Boards | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board?projectKeyOrId={key}` |
| Board config + WIP limits | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/configuration` |
| Sprints for board | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/sprint` |
| Issues in sprint | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog` |
| Project versions | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version` |
| Users | `GET /rest/api/3/user/search?query={name}` |
| All statuses | `GET /rest/api/3/status` |
| Cursor-based full export | `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` |

---

## What Jira Does NOT Provide via API

These metrics must be fully derived from raw data:

| Metric | Must Compute From |
|--------|------------------|
| Velocity | Sprint issues + story points + sprint dates |
| Burndown | Issues + story points + changelog over time |
| Cumulative Flow Diagram | Changelog status transitions, bucketed by status + date |
| Cycle time | Changelog first "In Progress" → resolutiondate |
| Lead time | `created` → `resolutiondate` |
| Time in status | Consecutive changelog entries |
| Throughput | Count of issues resolved per period (JQL) |
| Sprint scope change | Sprint changelog entries after sprint start |
| PR review time | Not in Jira — use GitHub API |
| Rework rate | Status changes from Done → active in changelog |

```
histories[] {
  id
  author { accountId, displayName, emailAddress }
  created    ← ISO-8601 timestamp of change
  items[] {
    field        ← field name
    fieldId      ← field ID
    from / fromString  ← previous value
    to / toString      ← new value
  }
}
```

### Fields Tracked in Changelog

| Field | Use For |
|-------|---------|
| `status` | **Time in status, cycle time** |
| `assignee` | Handoff count, reassignment frequency |
| `priority` | Priority escalations during sprint |
| `Story Points` | Estimation changes (re-pointing) |
| `Sprint` | Issues moved between sprints — **scope change** |
| `Fix Version` | Release scope changes |
| `Flagged` | Impediment/blocker tracking |
| `resolution` | Resolution date reconstruction |
| `summary` | Title rewrites (ticket quality signal) |
| `issuetype` | Type upgrades (bug discovered mid-story) |
| `duedate` | Due date slippage |
| `parent` | Sub-task re-parenting |

> **Critical**: Changelog does NOT record initial values. Combine `issue.created` + initial `issue.fields.status` with changelog to reconstruct full state history.

### Calculating Time in Status
```
1. Record (created_at, initial_status) from issue object
2. Sort changelog.histories by created timestamp
3. For each status change: time_in_status = next_change.created - current_change.created
4. Current status duration = now() - last_status_change.created
```

### Derived Flow Metrics from Changelog

| Metric | Calculation |
|--------|-------------|
| Lead time | `resolutiondate` - `created` |
| Cycle time | First "In Progress" timestamp (changelog) → `resolutiondate` |
| Time in code review | Sum of time in "In Review" / "Code Review" status |
| Blocked time | Sum of time in "Blocked" / "On Hold" OR when `Flagged = Impediment` |
| Rework rate | Status moved from Done category → active category (reopens) |
| Handoff count | Distinct assignee changes in changelog |
| Scope change | Sprint field changes after sprint `startDate` |
| Estimation changes | Story point changes in changelog |
| Re-points frequency | Count of `Story Points` changelog entries |

---

## Worklogs

`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog`

### Fields
- `author.accountId`, `author.displayName`
- `started` — when work was done (user-entered)
- `timeSpentSeconds` — use this for calculations
- `comment` — work description
- `created`, `updated`

### Incremental Sync Pattern
```
GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}  → IDs of updated worklogs
POST /rest/api/3/worklog/list                     → bulk fetch by IDs
```

### What Worklogs Enable
- Hours logged per developer per issue/sprint/epic
- Actual vs. estimated time by developer
- Developer capacity utilization over time
- After-hours logging detection (via `started` timestamp)

---

## Sprints

`GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/sprint?state=active,future,closed`

### Sprint Fields
- `id`, `name`, `state` — active/future/closed
- `startDate`, `endDate` — planned
- `completeDate` — actual close (populated when sprint closed)
- `goal` — sprint goal text
- `originBoardId`

### Sprint-Level Metrics (all derived from issue data — no native API)

| Metric | How to Compute |
|--------|----------------|
| Committed points | Story points for issues in sprint at `startDate` (use changelog to reconstruct) |
| Completed points | Story points for issues with status.category = done by sprint `endDate` |
| Completion rate | `completed / committed` |
| Scope added | Issues where sprint field changed TO this sprint in changelog after `startDate` |
| Scope removed | Issues where sprint field changed AWAY in changelog after `startDate` |
| Spillover | Issues in sprint, not completed by `endDate` |
| Velocity | Average completed points across last N sprints |

> **No official API for velocity/burndown/CFD on Jira Cloud.** Everything must be derived from raw issue + changelog data. Atlassian ticket JRACLOUD-96617 remains open.

---

## Boards

`GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{boardId}/configuration`

### Board Config
- `type` — scrum/kanban
- `columnConfig.columns[]` — each column:
  - `name` — column display name
  - `statuses[]` — workflow statuses mapped to this column
  - `min` / `max` — WIP limits
- `estimation.field.fieldId` — story_points or originalEstimate
- `filter.id` — JQL filter defining board scope
- `ranking.rankCustomFieldId`

### WIP Violations
Board config `max` limits + actual issue counts per status column = WIP violation detection.

---

## Projects

- `key`, `name`, `projectTypeKey` — software/business/service_desk
- `simplified` — `true` = team-managed (next-gen), `false` = classic
- `lead.accountId`, `lead.displayName`
- `components[]`, `issueTypes[]`, `versions[]`
- `insight.totalIssueCount`, `insight.lastIssueUpdateTime`
- `projectCategory.name`

---

## Versions / Releases

`GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version`

- `name`, `description`
- `released` (boolean), `archived`, `overdue`
- `startDate`, `releaseDate`
- `issuesFixedCount`, `issuesAffectedCount` (from `/relatedIssueCounts` endpoint)

---

## Components

`GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/component`

- `name`, `description`
- `lead.accountId` — component owner
- `assigneeType` — PROJECT_DEFAULT/COMPONENT_LEAD/PROJECT_LEAD/UNASSIGNED
- `issueCount`

---

## Users

`GET /rest/api/3/user?accountId={id}`

- `accountId`, `displayName`, `emailAddress`
- `accountType` — atlassian/app/customer
- `active` (boolean)
- `timeZone` — IANA timezone (work hours analysis)
- `locale`

---

## Comments

`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment`

- `author.accountId`, `author.displayName`
- `body` — rich text (ADF v3)
- `created`, `updated`
- `jsdPublic` — Service Desk: visible to customer?

### What Comments Enable
- First response time: `first_comment.created - issue.created`
- Discussion depth per ticket
- Non-assignee participant identification

---

## JQL Search

`POST /rest/api/3/search`

### Useful JQL Patterns

```jql
-- Developer output in sprint
assignee = "accountId" AND sprint in closedSprints() AND status = Done

-- Cycle time data
project = PROJ AND status changed to "Done" DURING ("2024-01-01", "2024-12-31")

-- Bugs introduced vs. bugs fixed
issuetype = Bug AND creator = "accountId" AND created >= -30d
issuetype = Bug AND assignee = "accountId" AND resolutiondate >= -30d

-- Re-opened issues (rework)
status changed to "In Progress" after resolutiondate is not EMPTY

-- Blockers
labels = "blocked" OR flagged = "Impediment"

-- Scope creep
sprint changed DURING (sprintStart, sprintEnd) AND sprint = currentSprint()
```

### Functions
- `currentUser()`, `membersOf("group")`
- `openSprints()`, `closedSprints()`, `futureSprints()`
- `startOfDay()`, `startOfWeek(-1w)`, `startOfMonth(-1M)`

### Pagination Limits
- Max 100 results per page with `expand=changelog`
- Max 10,000 offset — use `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` (cursor-based) for full export

---

## Development Information (Unofficial)

Links to connected SCM tools (GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab):

```
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=stash&dataType=pullrequest
GET /rest/dev-status/1.0/issue/detail?issueId={id}&applicationType=github&dataType=branch
```

Returns: PR count, open/merged/declined, PR title/URL, branch name, last commit info.

> ⚠️ Internal/unofficial API — not supported, may break. Use as supplemental only. Primary Git data should come from GitHub API directly.

---

## Key Endpoints Quick Reference

| Purpose | Endpoint |
|---------|----------|
| Issue with full history | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?expand=changelog,names` |
| Bulk issue search | `POST /rest/api/3/search` |
| Full changelog (paginated) | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/changelog` |
| Worklogs | `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/worklog` |
| Incremental worklog sync | `GET /rest/api/3/worklog/updated?since={epochMs}` |
| All custom fields | `GET /rest/api/3/field` |
| Boards | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board?projectKeyOrId={key}` |
| Board config + WIP limits | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/configuration` |
| Sprints for board | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/board/{id}/sprint` |
| Issues in sprint | `GET /rest/agile/1.0/sprint/{id}/issue?fields=*all&expand=changelog` |
| Project versions | `GET /rest/api/3/project/{key}/version` |
| Users | `GET /rest/api/3/user/search?query={name}` |
| All statuses | `GET /rest/api/3/status` |
| Cursor-based full export | `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` |

---

## What Jira Does NOT Provide via API

These metrics must be fully derived from raw data:

| Metric | Must Compute From |
|--------|------------------|
| Velocity | Sprint issues + story points + sprint dates |
| Burndown | Issues + story points + changelog over time |
| Cumulative Flow Diagram | Changelog status transitions, bucketed by status + date |
| Cycle time | Changelog first "In Progress" → resolutiondate |
| Lead time | `created` → `resolutiondate` |
| Time in status | Consecutive changelog entries |
| Throughput | Count of issues resolved per period (JQL) |
| Sprint scope change | Sprint changelog entries after sprint start |
| PR review time | Not in Jira — use GitHub API |
| Rework rate | Status changes from Done → active in changelog |

input.content:
# Metrics Catalog

All metrics the platform tracks, organized by source and complexity.
Raw data comes from GitHub API and Jira API — see integration docs for field-level detail.

---

## GitHub — Raw Metrics

### Commit Activity
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Commits per day/week/month | `/stats/contributors` weekly breakdown |
| Commit frequency (days active) | Punch card data |
| Working hours distribution | `stats/punch_card` — hourly by day of week |
| After-hours commits | `committed_at` or `authored_at` hour analysis |
| Commit message quality | Body presence, length, conventional commit format |
| Signed commits rate | `verification.verified` per commit |
| Merge commit ratio | `parents.length > 1` |
| Files changed per commit | `files[].status` counts |
| Lines added per commit | `stats.additions` |
| Lines deleted per commit | `stats.deletions` |
| Net code contribution | `additions - deletions` |

### Pull Request Metrics
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| PRs opened per period | PR `created_at` |
| PRs merged per period | PR `merged_at` |
| PRs abandoned (closed without merge) | `closed_at` where `merged = false` |
| PR draft rate | `draft = true` |
| PR size — lines | `additions + deletions` |
| PR size — files | `changed_files` |
| PR size — commits | `commits` |
| PR cycle time | `merged_at - created_at` |
| PR time in draft | `ready_for_review_at - created_at` (from timeline) |
| PR time to first review | First `review.submitted_at - pr.created_at` |
| PR time to merge after approval | `merged_at - last_approval.submitted_at` |
| PR review cycles | Count of CHANGES_REQUESTED → push → re-review |
| PR merge method | `squash / merge / rebase` distribution |
| PR with linked issues | `closingIssuesReferences` count |
| First-time contributor PRs | `author_association = FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR` |

### Code Review Metrics
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Reviews given per period | `review.submitted_at` by reviewer |
| Review state distribution | APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / COMMENTED ratio |
| Review response time | `review.submitted_at - review_requested_at` (timeline) |
| Inline comments left | `review_comments` count |
| Review thread resolution rate | `reviewThreads.isResolved / total` |
| Stale comment rate | `reviewThreads.isOutdated` |
| Reactions on review comments | 👍 👎 distribution |
| Reviews on own team's PRs vs. others | Cross-repo review detection |

### Repository & Code Quality
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Language distribution | `/languages` bytes per language |
| Open issues count | `repository.open_issues_count` |
| Fork count | `repository.forks_count` |
| Stars | `repository.stargazers_count` |
| Code scanning alerts by severity | Code scanning API |
| Secret scanning bypasses | `push_protection_bypassed` |
| Dependabot alert severity distribution | Dependabot alerts API |
| Dependency vulnerabilities introduced via PR | Dependency review API |
| Time to fix security alert | `fixed_at - created_at` per alert |

### CI / GitHub Actions
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Build success rate | `workflow_run.conclusion` distribution |
| Build duration | `run_duration_ms` |
| Build queue time | `run_started_at - created_at` |
| Flaky test rate | Failed runs immediately followed by success |
| CI job duration per step | `job.steps[].completed_at - started_at` |
| Failed builds per developer | `workflow_run.actor` on failure |
| Pipeline reliability trend | Success rate over time |

### Releases & Deployments
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Release frequency | Published releases per period |
| Time between releases | `release.published_at` delta |
| Deployment frequency | Deployments to production environment |
| Deployment success rate | `deployment_status.state` |
| Lead time to production | PR `merged_at` → deployment `created_at` |
| Rollback rate | Deployments following a failure |

---

## Jira — Raw Metrics

### Ticket Activity
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Tickets created per period | `issue.created` |
| Tickets resolved per period | `issue.resolutiondate` |
| Tickets assigned per developer | `issue.assignee` |
| Ticket types distribution | `issuetype.name` |
| Priority distribution | `priority.name` |
| Bug rate | `issuetype = Bug` ratio |
| Bugs created by developer | `reporter = dev AND issuetype = Bug` |
| Bugs fixed by developer | `assignee = dev AND issuetype = Bug AND status = Done` |
| Tickets reopened | Status changed from Done → active (changelog) |

### Time & Estimation
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Lead time per ticket | `resolutiondate - created` |
| Cycle time per ticket | First "In Progress" (changelog) → `resolutiondate` |
| Time in each status | Consecutive changelog status entries |
| Time in code review | Time in review/PR status columns |
| Blocked time | Time in "Blocked" status or `Flagged = Impediment` |
| Original estimate (story points) | `timetracking.originalEstimateSeconds` or story points custom field |
| Actual time logged | `timetracking.timeSpentSeconds` |
| Estimation accuracy | `workratio` = `(timeSpent / originalEstimate) * 100` |
| Time to first response | `first_comment.created - issue.created` |
| Due date hit rate | `resolutiondate <= duedate` |

### Sprint / Agile
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Story points committed per sprint | Sprint start snapshot (changelog) |
| Story points completed per sprint | Done issues at sprint end |
| Sprint completion rate | `completed / committed` |
| Scope added mid-sprint | Sprint field changes after `startDate` |
| Scope removed mid-sprint | Sprint field changes away from sprint |
| Spillover count | Incomplete issues at sprint end |
| Velocity (rolling average) | Completed points per sprint × N sprints |
| Sprint goal attainment | Committed epics/stories completed |
| Re-pointing frequency | Story point changelog entries |

### Collaboration & Process
| Metric | Source |
|--------|--------|
| Handoff count per ticket | Distinct assignee changes (changelog) |
| Comment volume per ticket | `comments` count |
| WIP violations | Issues per status vs. board column max |
| Sub-task completion rate | Closed sub-tasks / total sub-tasks |
| Issue link complexity | Total links (blocks/relates/clones) per issue |
| Dependency bottlenecks | Count of "blocked by" links on issues |
| Epic progress | Completed story points / total per epic |

---

## Cross-Source Metrics (GitHub + Jira Combined)

These require data from both sources, linked by issue key in commit messages / PR descriptions.

| Metric | Formula | Sources |
|--------|---------|---------|
| **End-to-end lead time** | `PR.merged_at - Jira.issue.created` | Jira created + GH merged |
| **Ticket-to-code time** | First commit timestamp - `Jira.issue.created` | GH commit + Jira created |
| **Code-to-review time** | `PR.created_at - first_commit_in_PR.committed_at` | GH PR + GH commit |
| **Review-to-merge time** | `PR.merged_at - first_review.submitted_at` | GH PR + GH review |
| **Deploy frequency per sprint** | Deployments during sprint dates | GH deployments + Jira sprint |
| **Bugs per PR size** | Bug tickets linked to PRs, grouped by PR size | Jira bugs + GH PR |
| **Re-work attribution** | PRs opened for re-opened Jira issues | Jira changelog + GH PR |
| **Sprint velocity vs. code output** | Story points × PRs merged × lines changed | Jira sprint + GH commits |

---

## Complex / Derived Metrics

Built on top of raw data. These are the high-value platform differentiators.

### Collaboration Score
```
collaboration_score = weighted_average(
  reviews_given_ratio,         // reviews given / PRs merged by team
  review_comment_depth,        // avg inline comments per review
  review_thread_resolution,    // resolved threads rate
  cross_team_review_rate,      // reviewing outside own area
  jira_comment_participation,  // comments on others' tickets
  pr_response_time_score       // how fast review requests are answered
)
```
Range: 0–100. Normalized within company, then against global benchmark.

---

### Mean Time to Development (MTTD)
```
MTTD = avg(first_commit.committed_at - jira_issue.created)
```
Measures time from ticket creation to first line of code.
Segments: by developer, team, issue type, priority.

---

### Mean Time to Review (MTTR)
```
MTTR = avg(first_review.submitted_at - PR.created_at)
```
Separate from code review response time — this measures the team's review culture.

---

### Code Churn Rate
```
churn_rate = lines_deleted_within_N_days_of_writing / total_lines_written

// Alternative:
churn_rate = files_modified_back_within_2_weeks / total_files_changed
```
High churn = rework, unclear requirements, or exploratory work.

---

### Burnout Risk Index
```
burnout_risk = weighted_score(
  after_hours_ratio,           // commits/PRs outside 9-17 local time
  weekend_activity_rate,       // weekend commits ratio
  output_variance,             // week-over-week std deviation
  streak_length,               // consecutive high-output days
  declining_output_trend,      // 4-week rolling average slope
  pr_cycle_time_increase,      // slowing down signal
  jira_blocked_time_increase   // more blockers = more frustration
)
```
Scale: LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / CRITICAL
Comparison: personal baseline + team average + global percentile.

---

### Velocity Consistency Score
```
consistency = 1 - (stddev(weekly_output) / mean(weekly_output))
```
Where output = story points + PRs merged + commit count (normalized/weighted).
High score = predictable, reliable delivery. Low = erratic.

---

### Review Quality Score
```
review_quality = weighted_score(
  comment_to_approval_ratio,   // substantive vs. rubber-stamp
  thread_resolution_follow_up, // reviewer follows up on their own threads
  actionable_comment_rate,     // comments that lead to code changes
  review_depth,                // lines reviewed / PR size
  review_cycle_prevention      // lower CHANGES_REQUESTED rate = better upfront reviews
)
```

---

### Impact Score
```
impact_score = weighted_composite(
  delivery_output,     // story points + tickets resolved (normalized)
  code_contribution,   // net lines + files (quality-adjusted by churn)
  review_contribution, // reviews given + quality score
  collaboration,       // collaboration score
  reliability,         // velocity consistency + CI pass rate
  security_hygiene,    // alert resolution speed, no bypasses
  mentorship_signal    // first-time contributor PR reviews
)
```
This is the top-level "developer value" score. Heavily caveated in UI — not a ranking, but a multidimensional profile.

---

### Seniority Classification (for benchmarks)
Used to match developers against global peers at same level.

```
seniority_level = classify(
  github_account_age,
  total_contribution_history,
  repository_complexity (stars, forks of contributed repos),
  code_review_authority (reviews requested from them),
  PR_size_and_scope,
  language_breadth
)
// Levels: JUNIOR | MID | SENIOR | STAFF | PRINCIPAL
```

---

### PR Health Score
```
pr_health = score(
  pr_size_normalized,          // smaller = better (up to a point)
  linked_to_ticket,            // has Jira link
  has_description,             // body length > threshold
  review_coverage,             // at least 1 substantive review
  ci_passed,                   // all checks green
  no_force_push,               // clean history
  branch_up_to_date            // rebased before merge
)
```

---

### Team Health Index
```
team_health = aggregate(
  average_cycle_time_trend,
  average_burnout_risk,
  sprint_completion_trend,
  wip_violation_rate,
  review_participation_rate,
  blocked_time_trend,
  scope_change_rate,
  velocity_trend
)
```

---

### Estimation Accuracy Score (Jira)
```
accuracy = 1 - abs(actual_time - estimated_time) / estimated_time
```
Aggregated per developer per sprint. Tracks improvement over time.
Separate scores for story points vs. time estimates.

---

## Global Benchmark Metrics

Percentile comparisons require sufficient sample size. Segmented by:
- Seniority level (JUNIOR/MID/SENIOR/STAFF/PRINCIPAL)
- Company size bracket (1–50 / 51–200 / 201–1000 / 1000+)
- Primary language/stack
- Team type (product / platform / infrastructure / data)

Available benchmarks:
- PR cycle time
- Review response time
- Commit frequency
- PR size (additions + deletions)
- Code churn rate
- Collaboration score
- Burnout risk index
- Velocity consistency
- CI pass rate
- Security alert resolution time

---

## Metrics That Require Additional Data

Planned for future phases:

| Metric | Requires |
|--------|---------|
| Code review comment quality (semantic) | LLM analysis of review text |
| Commit message quality score | NLP classification |
| Technical debt contribution | Code analysis tool (SonarQube, CodeClimate) |
| Test coverage contribution | Coverage reports from CI |
| Documentation contribution | Docs changed files in PR |
| On-call incident response time | PagerDuty / incident tool |
| Knowledge concentration risk | File ownership distribution (git blame) |
| Bus factor per codebase area | Contributor concentration per path |

content.text: Now update the roadmap to reference the catalog and add the full metrics list:

input.old_string:
### Raw Metrics
- [ ] Commits per period (day/week/month)
- [ ] PR open count, merge count, close-without-merge count
- [ ] PR cycle time (open → merge)
- [ ] PR size (lines changed)
- [ ] Code review participation (reviews given, comments left)
- [ ] Ticket velocity (story points completed per sprint)
- [ ] Sprint completion rate
- [ ] Estimation accuracy (estimated vs actual)
- [ ] After-hours activity detection

### Derived / Complex Metrics
- [ ] **Collaboration Score** — composite (reviews + comments + pair activity)
- [ ] **Mean Time to Development (MTTD)** — ticket created → first commit
- [ ] **Mean Time to Review (MTTR)** — PR opened → first review
- [ ] **Code Churn Rate** — lines rewritten vs net new
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** — after-hours density + output variance + streak analysis
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency** — week-over-week output variance
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth vs approvals ratio
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above

### Jira Raw Metrics (selected)

### Derived / Complex Metrics
- [ ] **Collaboration Score** — reviews + comments + cross-team participation
- [ ] **Mean Time to Development (MTTD)** — Jira created → first commit
- [ ] **Mean Time to Review (MTTR)** — PR opened → first review
- [ ] **Code Churn Rate** — lines rewritten within N days of writing
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** — after-hours + output variance + declining trend
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency Score** — stddev/mean of weekly output
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth vs. rubber-stamp approvals
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above
- [ ] **PR Health Score** — size + description + CI + review coverage
- [ ] **Team Health Index** — aggregate team-level composite
- [ ] **Estimation Accuracy Score** — per developer, per sprint, trended
- [ ] **Seniority Classification** — for global benchmark matching

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Feature Roadmap

## Phase 1 — Foundation (Current)

Core auth, multi-tenancy, and company management. No integrations yet.

### Completed
- [x] Auth system — GitHub + Google OAuth, JWT, Redis sessions
- [x] Multi-tenant company model — companies, members, roles (admin/member)
- [x] Invitation system — email-based, 72hr expiry, single-use
- [x] CSRF protection
- [x] Health checks, logging, rate limiting

---

## Phase 2 — Data Ingestion

Connect to external data sources and store raw developer activity.

### GitHub Integration
- [ ] GitHub OAuth app setup (org-level access)
- [ ] Webhook ingestion — push events, PR events, review events
- [ ] Polling fallback for historical data
- [ ] Store: commits, PRs, reviews, comments, diffs
- [ ] Contributor identity mapping (GitHub user → platform user)

### Jira Integration
- [ ] Jira OAuth 2.0 / API token setup
- [ ] Webhook ingestion — issue events, sprint events
- [ ] Historical data backfill
- [ ] Store: issues, sprints, worklogs, transitions, story points
- [ ] Jira user → platform user mapping

### Data Pipeline
- [ ] Queue system for ingestion jobs (BullMQ or similar)
- [ ] Idempotent event processing
- [ ] Raw event storage (append-only log)
- [ ] Normalized data layer on top of raw events

---

## Phase 3 — Metrics Engine

Calculate raw stats and derived complex metrics from ingested data.

### Raw Metrics
- [ ] Commits per period (day/week/month)
- [ ] PR open count, merge count, close-without-merge count
- [ ] PR cycle time (open → merge)
- [ ] PR size (lines changed)
- [ ] Code review participation (reviews given, comments left)
- [ ] Ticket velocity (story points completed per sprint)
- [ ] Sprint completion rate
- [ ] Estimation accuracy (estimated vs actual)
- [ ] After-hours activity detection

### Derived / Complex Metrics
- [ ] **Collaboration Score** — composite (reviews + comments + pair activity)
- [ ] **Mean Time to Development (MTTD)** — ticket created → first commit
- [ ] **Mean Time to Review (MTTR)** — PR opened → first review
- [ ] **Code Churn Rate** — lines rewritten vs net new
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** — after-hours density + output variance + streak analysis
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency** — week-over-week output variance
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth vs approvals ratio
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above

### Benchmarking Data
- [ ] Anonymized aggregation pipeline (B2B data)
- [ ] Public GitHub profile scraper for global benchmarks
- [ ] Seniority classification model (based on account age, repo stars, contribution history)
- [ ] Percentile calculation per metric per seniority band

---

## Phase 4 — Dashboard & Visualizations

Frontend views for all comparison modes and data presentations.

### Views
- [ ] Single developer profile dashboard
- [ ] Dev vs Dev comparison
- [ ] Dev vs Team comparison
- [ ] Dev vs Global benchmark
- [ ] Team overview dashboard
- [ ] Team vs Team comparison
- [ ] Time-series views for all of the above

### Presentation Formats
- [ ] Table view (sortable, filterable, exportable)
- [ ] Line/bar charts — trends over time
- [ ] Radar chart — multi-metric profile
- [ ] Heatmap — activity calendar (GitHub-style)
- [ ] Metric cards — summary KPIs

---

## Phase 5 — AI Layer

Natural language analysis and recommendations on top of metrics.

- [ ] Per-developer AI narrative report (weekly/monthly)
- [ ] Short actionable tips per developer
- [ ] Anomaly detection with AI explanation
- [ ] Benchmark comparison narratives
- [ ] Team health summary with AI commentary
- [ ] Natural language query interface ("Who is at burnout risk?")

---

## Phase 6 — B2C Features

Individual developer self-service, independent of company accounts.

- [ ] Personal developer account (standalone, no company required)
- [ ] Connect personal GitHub account
- [ ] Personal stats dashboard
- [ ] Shareable performance card — generated image (CV insert, LinkedIn, GitHub README)
- [ ] Public developer profile page (opt-in)
- [ ] Personal AI analysis report

---

## Future / Backlog

- GitLab integration
- Linear / Shortcut / Azure DevOps integration
- Slack collaboration signals
- PR quality analysis (LLM-based code review scoring)
- 1:1 meeting prep reports for managers
- Performance review automation assist
- Mobile app
- Webhooks / API for customers to pull their own data
- SSO / SAML for enterprise
